revision.comment: 新規投稿
revision.text:
{{神社 |名称 = 意賀美神社 |画像 = [[ファイル:OgamiJinjaHaiden|250px|意賀美神社拝殿]]
拝殿。本殿はこの拝殿内にある。 |所在地 = 岸和田市土生滝町17 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|24|57.54|N|135|24|38.94|E|scale:30000}} |祭神 = 闇意賀美大神、市杵島姫命、八大竜王・牛頭天王、井滝岩神 |社格 = 式内小社 |創建 = [[天平]]以前 |本殿 = 破風造 |別名 = 雨降明神 |例祭 = 10月5日 |神事 = }} '''意賀美神社'''（おがみじんじゃ）は[[大阪府]][[岸和田市]]にある[[神社]]。[[和泉国]]の[[式内社]]である。阿間河滝村・土生滝村の産土神であり、水利の要所に祀られている。 == 歴史 == [[聖武天皇]]の御代、[[天平]]四年（[[732年]]）の夏に大旱魃があった際、祈雨 の霊験があったといわれる。 また、[[陽成天皇]]の御代、[[元慶]]八年（[[884年]]）に畿内で大旱魃があった 際、[[菅原道真]]を[[奉幣]]使として祈雨したところ霊験があったといわれ、こ れ以降、雨降明神と言われるようになったという。 [[明治]]三九年（[[1906年]]）八月十四日、内務省神社・宗教両局長依命通牒によるいわゆる「神社合祀」に基づき、[[明治]]四十年十一月十一日、土生滝にあった古元神社および一瀬神社が村社であった意賀美神社に合祀された。 == 文化財 == * 神額 - 花山院厚卿筆 == 境内社 == * 厳島神社 * 意賀美荒神社 == 神社周辺 == 境内は神於山（こうのやま）の北側の裾にあり、目の前を流れる津田川にそそぎ込む支流に滝があ り、これが「雨降りの滝」である。 この地域は比較的内陸にあるため、瀬戸内海気候に似て、梅雨後の降雨が少ないという特徴がある。 ところが境内を含む周辺は湿潤な環境を示し、この付近には珍しい暖地の広葉樹種がみられ、 豊かな自然環境の中に位置する神社である。 境内の社叢はコジイとミミズバイが優先する常緑広葉樹林で、ホルトノキ、タイミンタチバナ、ツルコウジなどの常緑樹もみられる。[[昭和]]43年（[[1968年]]）には岸和田市の天然記念物に指 定されている。大阪府の「みどりの百選」に指定されており、[[平成]]元年には 大阪府の自然環境保全地域にも選ばれている。 しかし意賀美神社を含む神於山地区は、ここ50年で自然環境破壊が進んだ。現在、 自然再生推進法に基づき、農林水産省のもとで「神於山地区自然再生全体構想」 （平成16年10月）、「神於山地区自然再生事業実施計画」（平成17年6月）に基づ いた自然再生事業が始まっている。 == 交通 == * バスは南海電鉄からは岸和田駅前から、JR阪和線からは東岸和田駅から、南海ウイングバス南部にて0,41,42,43系統で宮の台下車。本数は一時間に2本程度である。 * 自動車の場合は[[阪和自動車道]]の貝塚IC（最寄）または岸和田和泉ICから国道170号線を使う。 == ギャラリー == ファイル:OgamiJinjaWaterfall.jpg|意賀美神社の雨降の滝 ==外部リンク== {{Commons|Category:Ogami-jinja}} {{DEFAULTSORT:おかみしんしや}} [[Category:式内小社]] [[Category:大阪府の神社]] [[Category:岸和田市]] {{Shinto-stub}}

revision.text:
{{神社 |名称 = 意賀美神社 |画像 = [[ファイル:OgamiJinjaHaiden.jpg|250px|意賀美神社拝殿]]
拝殿。本殿はこの拝殿内にある。 |所在地 = 岸和田市土生滝町17 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|24|57.54|N|135|24|38.94|E|scale:30000}} |祭神 = 闇意賀美大神、市杵島姫命、八大竜王・牛頭天王、井滝岩神 |社格 = 式内小社 |創建 = [[天平]]以前 |本殿 = 破風造 |別名 = 雨降明神 |例祭 = 10月5日 |神事 = }} [[ファイル:OgamiJinjaWaterfall.jpg|thumb|180px|right|意賀美神社の雨降の滝]] '''意賀美神社'''（おがみじんじゃ）は[[大阪府]][[岸和田市]]にある[[神社]]。[[和泉国]]の[[式内社]]である。阿間河滝村・土生滝村の産土神であり、水利の要所に祀られている。 == 歴史 == [[聖武天皇]]の治世、[[天平]]四年（[[732年]]）の夏に大旱魃があった際、祈雨 の霊験があったといわれる。 また、[[陽成天皇]]の御代、[[元慶]]八年（[[884年]]）に畿内で大旱魃があった 際、[[菅原道真]]を[[奉幣]]使として祈雨したところ霊験があったといわれ、こ れ以降、雨降明神と言われるようになったという。 [[明治]]39年（[[1906年]]）8月14日、内務省神社・宗教両局長依命通牒によるいわゆる「神社合祀」に基づき、[[明治]]40年11月11日、土生滝にあった古元神社および一瀬神社が村社であった意賀美神社に合祀された。 == 文化財 == * 神額 - 花山院厚卿筆 == 境内社 == * 厳島神社 * 意賀美荒神社 == 神社周辺 == 境内は神於山（こうのやま）の北側の裾にある。 境内の前を流れる津田川にそそぎ込む支流に滝があり、これが「雨降りの滝」である。 意賀美神社のある地域は比較的内陸にあるため、瀬戸内海気候に似て、梅雨後の降雨量が少ないという特徴がある。 ところが境内を含む周辺は湿潤な環境を有しており、この付近には珍しい暖地の広葉樹種がみられ、 豊かな自然環境の中に位置する神社である。 境内の社叢はコジイとミミズバイが優先する常緑広葉樹林で、ホルトノキ、タイミンタチバナ、ツルコウジなどの常緑樹もみられる。[[昭和]]43年（[[1968年]]）には岸和田市の天然記念物に指 定されている。大阪府の「みどりの百選」に指定されており、[[平成]]元年には 大阪府の自然環境保全地域にも選ばれている。 しかし意賀美神社を含む神於山地区は、ここ50年で自然環境破壊が進んでしまった。現在、 自然再生推進法に基づき、農林水産省のもとで「神於山地区自然再生全体構想」 （平成16年10月）、「神於山地区自然再生事業実施計画」（平成17年6月）に基づ いた自然再生事業が始まっている。 == 交通 == * バスは南海電鉄からは岸和田駅前から、JR阪和線からは東岸和田駅から、南海ウイングバス南部にて0,41,42,43系統で宮の台下車。本数は一時間に2本程度である。 * 自動車の場合は[[阪和自動車道]]の貝塚IC（最寄）または岸和田和泉ICから国道170号線を使う。 {{Commons|Category:Ogami-jinja, Kishiwada}} {{DEFAULTSORT:おかみしんしや}} [[Category:式内小社]] [[Category:大阪府の神社]] [[Category:岸和田市]] {{Shinto-stub}}

revision.comment: /* 神社周辺 */
revision.text:
{{神社 |名称 = 意賀美神社 |画像 = [[ファイル:OgamiJinjaHaiden.jpg|250px|意賀美神社拝殿]]
拝殿。本殿はこの拝殿内にある。 |所在地 = 岸和田市土生滝町17 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|24|57.54|N|135|24|38.94|E|scale:30000}} |祭神 = 闇意賀美大神、市杵島姫命、八大竜王・牛頭天王、井滝岩神 |社格 = 式内小社、村社 |創建 = [[天平]]以前 |本殿 = 破風造 |別名 = 雨降明神 |例祭 = 10月5日 |神事 = }} [[ファイル:OgamiJinjaWaterfall.jpg|thumb|180px|right|意賀美神社の雨降の滝]] '''意賀美神社'''（おがみじんじゃ）は[[大阪府]][[岸和田市]]にある[[神社]]。[[和泉国]]の[[式内社]]である。阿間河滝村・土生滝村の産土神であり、水利の要所に祀られている。 == 歴史 == [[聖武天皇]]の治世、[[天平]]四年（[[732年]]）の夏に大旱魃があった際、祈雨 の霊験があったといわれる。 また、[[陽成天皇]]の御代、[[元慶]]八年（[[884年]]）に畿内で大旱魃があった 際、[[菅原道真]]を[[奉幣]]使として祈雨したところ霊験があったといわれ、こ れ以降、雨降明神と言われるようになったという。 [[明治]]39年（[[1906年]]）8月14日、内務省神社・宗教両局長依命通牒によるいわゆる「神社合祀」に基づき、[[明治]]40年11月11日、土生滝にあった古元神社および一瀬神社が[[村社]]であった意賀美神社に合祀された。 == 文化財 == * 神額 - 花山院厚卿筆 == 境内社 == * 厳島神社 * 意賀美荒神社 == 神社周辺 == 境内は神於山（こうのやま）の北側の裾にある。 境内の前を流れる津田川にそそぎ込む支流に滝があり、これが「雨降りの滝」である。 意賀美神社のある地域は比較的内陸にあるため、瀬戸内海気候に似て、梅雨後の降雨量が少ないという特徴がある。 ところが境内を含む周辺は湿潤な環境を有しており、この付近には珍しい暖地の広葉樹種がみられ、 豊かな自然環境の中に位置する神社である。 境内の社叢はコジイとミミズバイが優先する常緑広葉樹林で、ホルトノキ、タイミンタチバナ、ツルコウジなどの常緑樹もみられる。[[昭和]]43年（[[1968年]]）には岸和田市の天然記念物に指 定されている。[[大阪みどりの百選]]に選定されており{{citeweb|url= http://www.pref.osaka.lg.jp/midorikikaku/toshiryokka/hyakusenn.html|title= 大阪みどりの百選|publisher=大阪府 |accessdate=2016-12-23}}、[[平成]]元年には 大阪府の自然環境保全地域にも選ばれている。 しかし意賀美神社を含む神於山地区は、ここ50年で自然環境破壊が進んでしまった。現在、 自然再生推進法に基づき、農林水産省のもとで「神於山地区自然再生全体構想」 （平成16年10月）、「神於山地区自然再生事業実施計画」（平成17年6月）に基づ いた自然再生事業が始まっている。 == 交通 == * バスは南海電鉄からは岸和田駅前から、JR阪和線からは東岸和田駅から、南海ウイングバス南部にて0,41,42,43系統で宮の台下車。本数は一時間に2本程度である。 * 自動車の場合は[[阪和自動車道]]の貝塚IC（最寄）または岸和田和泉ICから国道170号線を使う。 {{大阪みどりの百選}} {{Commons|Category:Ogami-jinja, Kishiwada}} {{DEFAULTSORT:おかみしんしや}} [[Category:和泉国の式内社]] [[Category:大阪府の神社]] [[Category:岸和田市の歴史]] [[Category:大阪府の旧村社]] {{Shinto-stub}}

拝殿| |所在地 = 岸和田市土生滝町17 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|24|57.54|N|135|24|38.94|E|scale:30000}} |祭神 = 闇意賀美大神

八大竜王・牛頭天王
井滝岩神 |社格 = [[式内社]]（小）
旧村社 |創建 = [[天平]]以前 |本殿 = 破風造 |別名 = 雨降明神 |例祭 = 10月5日 |神事 = }} [[ファイル:OgamiJinjaWaterfall.jpg|thumb|180px|right|意賀美神社の雨降の滝]] '''意賀美神社'''（おがみじんじゃ）は、[[大阪府]][[岸和田市]]にある神社。[[式内社]]で[[旧社格]]は村社。阿間河滝村・土生滝村の産土神であり、水利の要所に祀られている。 == 歴史 == [[聖武天皇]]の治世、[[天平]]四年（[[732年]]）の夏に大旱魃があった際、祈雨 の霊験があったといわれる。 また、[[陽成天皇]]の御代、[[元慶]]八年（[[884年]]）に畿内で大旱魃があった 際、[[菅原道真]]を[[奉幣]]使として祈雨したところ霊験があったといわれ、こ れ以降、雨降明神と言われるようになったという。 [[明治]]39年（[[1906年]]）8月14日、内務省神社・宗教両局長依命通牒によるいわゆる「神社合祀」に基づき、[[明治]]40年11月11日、土生滝にあった古元神社および一瀬神社が[[村社]]であった意賀美神社に合祀された。 == 文化財 == * 神額 - 花山院厚卿筆 == 境内社 == * 厳島神社 * 意賀美荒神社 == 神社周辺 == 境内は神於山（こうのやま）の北側の裾にある。 境内の前を流れる津田川にそそぎ込む支流に滝があり、これが「雨降りの滝」である。 意賀美神社のある地域は比較的内陸にあるため、瀬戸内海気候に似て、梅雨後の降雨量が少ないという特徴がある。 ところが境内を含む周辺は湿潤な環境を有しており、この付近には珍しい暖地の広葉樹種がみられ、 豊かな自然環境の中に位置する神社である。 境内の社叢はコジイとミミズバイが優先する常緑広葉樹林で、ホルトノキ、タイミンタチバナ、ツルコウジなどの常緑樹もみられる。[[昭和]]43年（[[1968年]]）には岸和田市の天然記念物に指 定されている。[[大阪みどりの百選]]に選定されており{{citeweb|url= http://www.pref.osaka.lg.jp/midorikikaku/toshiryokka/hyakusenn.html|title= 大阪みどりの百選|publisher=大阪府 |accessdate=2016-12-23}}、[[平成]]元年には 大阪府の自然環境保全地域にも選ばれている。 しかし意賀美神社を含む神於山地区は、ここ50年で自然環境破壊が進んでしまった。現在、 自然再生推進法に基づき、農林水産省のもとで「神於山地区自然再生全体構想」 （平成16年10月）、「神於山地区自然再生事業実施計画」（平成17年6月）に基づ いた自然再生事業が始まっている。 == 交通 == * バスは南海電鉄からは岸和田駅前から、JR阪和線からは東岸和田駅から、南海ウイングバス南部にて0,41,42,43系統で宮の台下車。本数は一時間に2本程度である。 * 自動車の場合は[[阪和自動車道]]の貝塚IC（最寄）または岸和田和泉ICから国道170号線を使う。 == 脚注 == {{reflist}} {{大阪みどりの百選}} {{Commons|Category:Ogami-jinja, Kishiwada}} {{DEFAULTSORT:おかみしんしや}} [[Category:和泉国の式内社]] [[Category:大阪府の神社]] [[Category:岸和田市の歴史]] [[Category:岸和田市の建築物]] [[Category:大阪府の旧村社]] {{Shinto-stub}}

井滝岩神 |社格 = [[式内社]]（小）
旧村社 |創建 = [[天平]]以前 |本殿 = 破風造 |別名 = 雨降明神 |例祭 = 10月5日 |神事 = }} [[ファイル:OgamiJinjaWaterfall.jpg|thumb|180px|right|意賀美神社の雨降の滝]] '''意賀美神社'''（おがみじんじゃ）は、[[大阪府]][[岸和田市]]にある神社。[[式内社]]で[[旧社格]]は村社。阿間河滝村・土生滝村の産土神であり、水利の要所に祀られている。 == 歴史 == [[聖武天皇]]の治世、[[天平]]四年（[[732年]]）の夏に大旱魃があった際、祈雨 の霊験があったといわれる。 また、[[陽成天皇]]の御代、[[元慶]]八年（[[884年]]）に畿内で大旱魃があった 際、[[菅原道真]]を[[奉幣]]使として祈雨したところ霊験があったといわれ、こ れ以降、雨降明神と言われるようになったという。 [[明治]]39年（[[1906年]]）8月14日、内務省神社・宗教両局長依命通牒によるいわゆる「神社合祀」に基づき、[[明治]]40年11月11日、土生滝にあった古元神社および一瀬神社が[[村社]]であった意賀美神社に合祀された。 == 文化財 == * 神額 - 花山院厚卿筆 == 境内社 == * 厳島神社 * 意賀美荒神社 == 神社周辺 == 境内は神於山（こうのやま）の北側の裾にある。 境内の前を流れる津田川にそそぎ込む支流に滝があり、これが「雨降りの滝」である。 意賀美神社のある地域は比較的内陸にあるため、瀬戸内海気候に似て、梅雨後の降雨量が少ないという特徴がある。 ところが境内を含む周辺は湿潤な環境を有しており、この付近には珍しい暖地の広葉樹種がみられ、 豊かな自然環境の中に位置する神社である。 境内の社叢はコジイとミミズバイが優先する常緑広葉樹林で、ホルトノキ、タイミンタチバナ、ツルコウジなどの常緑樹もみられる。[[昭和]]43年（[[1968年]]）には岸和田市の天然記念物に指 定されている。[[大阪みどりの百選]]に選定されており{{citeweb|url= http://www.pref.osaka.lg.jp/midorikikaku/toshiryokka/hyakusenn.html|title= 大阪みどりの百選|publisher=大阪府 |accessdate=2016-12-23}}、[[平成]]元年には 大阪府の自然環境保全地域にも選ばれている。 しかし意賀美神社を含む神於山地区は、ここ50年で自然環境破壊が進んでしまった。現在、 自然再生推進法に基づき、農林水産省のもとで「神於山地区自然再生全体構想」 （平成16年10月）、「神於山地区自然再生事業実施計画」（平成17年6月）に基づ いた自然再生事業が始まっている。 == 交通 == * バスは南海電鉄からは岸和田駅前から、JR阪和線からは東岸和田駅から、南海ウイングバス南部にて0,41,42,43系統で宮の台下車。本数は一時間に2本程度である。 * 自動車の場合は[[阪和自動車道]]の貝塚IC（最寄）または岸和田和泉ICから国道170号線を使う。 == 脚注 == {{reflist}} {{大阪みどりの百選}} {{Commons|Category:Ogami-jinja, Kishiwada}} {{DEFAULTSORT:おかみしんしや}} [[Category:和泉国の式内社]] [[Category:大阪府の神社]] [[Category:岸和田市の歴史]] [[Category:岸和田市の建築物]] [[Category:大阪府の旧村社]] {{Shinto-stub}}

{{神社 |名称 = 意賀美神社 |画像 = [[ファイル:OgamiJinjaHaiden.jpg|250px|意賀美神社拝殿]]
拝殿{{Maplink2|zoom=12|frame=yes|plain=yes|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|frame-height=220|type=point|frame-latitude=34.437|frame-longitude=135.40|coord={{coord2|34|24|57.54|N|135|24|38.94|E}}}} |所在地 = 岸和田市土生滝町17 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|24|57.54|N|135|24|38.94|E|scale:30000}} |祭神 = 闇意賀美大神

八大竜王・牛頭天王
井滝岩神 |社格 = [[式内社]]（小）
旧村社 |創建 = [[天平]]以前 |本殿 = 破風造 |別名 = 雨降明神 |例祭 = 10月5日 |神事 = }} [[ファイル:OgamiJinjaWaterfall.jpg|thumb|180px|right|意賀美神社の雨降の滝]] '''意賀美神社'''（おがみじんじゃ）は、[[大阪府]][[岸和田市]]にある神社。[[式内社]]で[[旧社格]]は村社。阿間河滝村・土生滝村の産土神であり、水利の要所に祀られている。 == 歴史 == [[聖武天皇]]の治世、[[天平]]四年（[[732年]]）の夏に大旱魃があった際、祈雨の霊験があったといわれる。また、[[陽成天皇]]の御代、[[元慶]]八年（[[884年]]）に畿内で大旱魃があった際、[[菅原道真]]を[[奉幣]]使として祈雨したところ霊験があったといわれ、これ以降、雨降明神と言われるようになったという。 [[明治]]39年（[[1906年]]）8月14日、内務省神社・宗教両局長依命通牒によるいわゆる「神社合祀」に基づき、[[明治]]40年11月11日、土生滝にあった古元神社および一瀬神社が[[村社]]であった意賀美神社に合祀された。 == 文化財 == * 神額 - 花山院厚卿筆 == 境内社 == * 厳島神社 * 意賀美荒神社 == 神社周辺 == 境内は[[神於山]]（こうのやま）の北側の裾にある。境内の前を流れる津田川にそそぎ込む支流に滝があり、これが「雨降りの滝」である。意賀美神社のある地域は比較的内陸にあるため、瀬戸内海気候に似て、梅雨後の降雨量が少ないという特徴がある。ところが境内を含む周辺は湿潤な環境を有しており、この付近には珍しい暖地の広葉樹種がみられ、豊かな自然環境の中に位置する神社である。 境内の社叢はコジイとミミズバイが優先する常緑広葉樹林で、ホルトノキ、タイミンタチバナ、ツルコウジなどの常緑樹もみられる。[[昭和]]43年（[[1968年]]）には岸和田市の天然記念物に指定されている。[[大阪みどりの百選]]に選定されており{{citeweb|url= http://www.pref.osaka.lg.jp/midorikikaku/toshiryokka/hyakusenn.html|title= 大阪みどりの百選|publisher=大阪府 |accessdate=2016-12-23}}、[[平成]]元年には大阪府の自然環境保全地域にも選ばれている。 しかし意賀美神社を含む神於山地区は、ここ50年で自然環境破壊が進んでしまった。現在、自然再生推進法に基づき、農林水産省のもとで「神於山地区自然再生全体構想」（平成16年10月）、「神於山地区自然再生事業実施計画」（平成17年6月）に基づいた自然再生事業が始まっている。 == 交通 == * バスは南海電鉄からは岸和田駅前から、JR阪和線からは東岸和田駅から、南海ウイングバス南部にて0,41,42,43系統で宮の台下車。本数は一時間に2本程度である。 * 自動車の場合は[[阪和自動車道]]の貝塚IC（最寄）または岸和田和泉ICから国道170号線を使う。 == 脚注 == {{reflist}} {{Commons|Category:Ogami-jinja, Kishiwada}} {{大阪みどりの百選}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:おかみしんしや}} [[Category:和泉国の式内社]] [[Category:大阪府の神社]] [[Category:岸和田市の歴史]] [[Category:岸和田市の建築物]] [[Category:大阪府の旧村社]]

revision.text:
{{Shrine | Name = Ogami Shrine (意賀美神社) | Image = [[File:OgamiJinjaHaiden.jpg|250px|意賀美神社拝殿]]
Haiden {{Maplink2|zoom=12|frame=yes|plain=yes|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|frame-height=220|type=point|frame-latitude=34.437|frame-longitude=135.40|coord={{coord|34|24|57.54|N|135|24|38.94|E}}}} | Location = 17 Habutaki-cho, Kishiwada City, Osaka Prefecture, Japan | Coordinates = {{Coord|34|24|57.54|N|135|24|38.94|E|region:JP-27_scale:30000|display=inline,title}} | Deities = Yamigami-no-Ōkami
Ichikishimahime-no-Mikoto
Hachidai Ryūō and Gozu Tennō
Itaki Iwa-no-Kami | Shrine_rank = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}} (Minor), Former Village Shrine | Foundation = Before the {{ill|Tenpyō era|ja|天平}} (729–749) | Main_building = {{ill|Hafu-zukuri|ja|破風造}} | Festival = October 5 }} '''Ogami Shrine''' (意賀美神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Kishiwada|ja|岸和田市}}, Osaka Prefecture, Japan. It is a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}} (Minor Shrine) and was classified as a village shrine under the {{ill|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|ja|近代社格制度}}. The shrine serves as the tutelary deity for the former Amakawataki and Habutaki villages and is located at an important water management site. ## History During the reign of {{ill|Emperor Shōmu|ja|聖武天皇}}, the shrine gained recognition in 732 CE (Tenpyō 4) for its efficacy in bringing rain during a severe drought. Similarly, during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Yōzei|ja|陽成天皇}} in 884 CE (Gangyō 8), {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|菅原道真}} offered prayers during another drought, and the shrine was revered for its rain-bringing miracles. Since then, the shrine has also been called the "Rainfall Deity Shrine." On August 14, 1906 (Meiji 39), under the {{ill|Shrine Consolidation Policy|ja|神社合祀}} directive, the {{ill|Furumoto Shrine|ja|古元神社}} and {{ill|Ichinose Shrine|ja|一瀬神社}}, both located in Habutaki, were merged with Ogami Shrine on November 11, 1906. ## Cultural Assets * Shrine plaque inscribed by {{ill|Kazanin Atsunaga|ja|花山院厚卿}} ## Auxiliary Shrines * Itsukushima Shrine * Ogami Kojin Shrine ## Shrine Surroundings The shrine is located at the northern foothills of {{ill|Kōnoyama|ja|神於山}}. The site includes the "Rainfall Waterfall" (雨降りの滝), which flows into a tributary of the Tsudagawa River. Due to its inland location, the region often experiences reduced rainfall after the rainy season, similar to the Seto Inland Sea climate. However, the shrine's surroundings are notably humid, supporting diverse vegetation, including rare warm-temperate broadleaf trees. The forest around the shrine consists of evergreen broadleaf species dominated by {{ill|Castanopsis cuspidata|ja|コジイ}} and {{ill|Elaeocarpus japonicus|ja|ミミズバイ}}, along with {{ill|Holtunok|ja|ホルトノキ}}, {{ill|Ardisia japonica|ja|タイミンタチバナ}}, and {{ill|Corchoropsis tomentosa|ja|ツルコウジ}}. This area was designated a Natural Monument by Kishiwada City in 1968 (Shōwa 43). It is also listed among the {{ill|Osaka 100 Best Green Sites|ja|大阪みどりの百選}} and was selected as an Osaka Prefecture Natural Conservation Area in 1989 (Heisei 1). However, the natural environment around the shrine, including Kōnoyama, has suffered significant degradation over the past 50 years. Restoration efforts are ongoing under the "Kōnoyama Area Natural Restoration Comprehensive Plan" (October 2004) and the "Kōnoyama Area Natural Restoration Project Implementation Plan" (June 2005), supervised by the Ministry of Agriculture, Forestry and Fisheries. ## Access - **By Bus**: From {{ill|Kishiwada Station|ja|岸和田駅}} (Nankai Electric Railway) or {{ill|Higashi-Kishiwada Station|ja|東岸和田駅}} (JR Hanwa Line), take the Nankai Wing Bus South on routes 0, 41, 42, or 43 to Miyano-dai. Buses run approximately twice per hour. - **By Car**: Use {{ill|Hanwa Expressway|ja|阪和自動車道}}, exiting at either Kaizuka IC or Kishiwada-Izumi IC, and follow National Route 170. ## References {{Reflist}} {{Commons|Category:Ogami-jinja, Kishiwada}} {{Osaka 100 Best Green Sites}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Ogami Shrine}} [[Category:Shikinaisha in Izumi Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Kishiwada]] [[Category:Buildings in Kishiwada]] [[Category:Former village shrines in Japan]] [[ja:意賀美神社]]

It is also listed among the {{ill|Osaka 100 Best Green Sites|ja|大阪みどりの百選}} and was selected as an Osaka Prefecture Natural Conservation Area in 1989 (Heisei 1). However, the natural environment around the shrine, including Kōnoyama, has suffered significant degradation over the past 50 years. Restoration efforts are ongoing under the "Kōnoyama Area Natural Restoration Comprehensive Plan" (October 2004) and the "Kōnoyama Area Natural Restoration Project Implementation Plan" (June 2005), supervised by the Ministry of Agriculture, Forestry and Fisheries. ## Access - **By Bus**: From {{ill|Kishiwada Station|ja|岸和田駅}} (Nankai Electric Railway) or {{ill|Higashi-Kishiwada Station|ja|東岸和田駅}} (JR Hanwa Line), take the Nankai Wing Bus South on routes 0, 41, 42, or 43 to Miyano-dai. Buses run approximately twice per hour. - **By Car**: Use {{ill|Hanwa Expressway|ja|阪和自動車道}}, exiting at either Kaizuka IC or Kishiwada-Izumi IC, and follow National Route 170. ## References {{Reflist}} {{Commons|Category:Ogami-jinja, Kishiwada}} {{Osaka 100 Best Green Sites}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Ogami Shrine}} [[Category:Shikinaisha in Izumi Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Kishiwada]] [[Category:Buildings in Kishiwada]] [[Category:Former village shrines in Japan]] [[Category:Https://ja.wikipedia.org/w/index.php?title=]][[ja:%E6%84%8F%E8%B3%80%E7%BE%8E%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE (%E5%B2%B8%E5%92%8C%E7%94%B0%E5%B8%82)]][[ja:&action=edit]] [[ja:意賀美神社]]

{{Shrine | Name = Ogami Shrine (意賀美神社) | Image = [[File:OgamiJinjaHaiden.jpg|250px|意賀美神社拝殿]]
Haiden {{Maplink2|zoom=12|frame=yes|plain=yes|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|frame-height=220|type=point|frame-latitude=34.437|frame-longitude=135.40|coord={{coord|34|24|57.54|N|135|24|38.94|E}}}} | Location = 17 Habutaki-cho, Kishiwada City, Osaka Prefecture, Japan | Coordinates = {{Coord|34|24|57.54|N|135|24|38.94|E|region:JP-27_scale:30000|display=inline,title}} | Deities = Yamigami-no-Ōkami
Ichikishimahime-no-Mikoto
Hachidai Ryūō and Gozu Tennō
Itaki Iwa-no-Kami | Shrine_rank = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}} (Minor), Former Village Shrine | Foundation = Before the {{ill|Tenpyō era|ja|天平}} (729–749) | Main_building = {{ill|Hafu-zukuri|ja|破風造}} | Festival = October 5 }} '''Ogami Shrine''' (意賀美神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Kishiwada|ja|岸和田市}}, Osaka Prefecture, Japan. It is a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}} (Minor Shrine) and was classified as a village shrine under the {{ill|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|ja|近代社格制度}}. The shrine serves as the tutelary deity for the former Amakawataki and Habutaki villages and is located at an important water management site. ## History During the reign of {{ill|Emperor Shōmu|ja|聖武天皇}}, the shrine gained recognition in 732 CE (Tenpyō 4) for its efficacy in bringing rain during a severe drought. Similarly, during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Yōzei|ja|陽成天皇}} in 884 CE (Gangyō 8), {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|菅原道真}} offered prayers during another drought, and the shrine was revered for its rain-bringing miracles. Since then, the shrine has also been called the "Rainfall Deity Shrine." On August 14, 1906 (Meiji 39), under the {{ill|Shrine Consolidation Policy|ja|神社合祀}} directive, the {{ill|Furumoto Shrine|ja|古元神社}} and {{ill|Ichinose Shrine|ja|一瀬神社}}, both located in Habutaki, were merged with Ogami Shrine on November 11, 1906. ## Cultural Assets * Shrine plaque inscribed by {{ill|Kazanin Atsunaga|ja|花山院厚卿}} ## Auxiliary Shrines * Itsukushima Shrine * Ogami Kojin Shrine ## Shrine Surroundings The shrine is located at the northern foothills of {{ill|Kōnoyama|ja|神於山}}. The site includes the "Rainfall Waterfall" (雨降りの滝), which flows into a tributary of the Tsudagawa River. Due to its inland location, the region often experiences reduced rainfall after the rainy season, similar to the Seto Inland Sea climate. However, the shrine's surroundings are notably humid, supporting diverse vegetation, including rare warm-temperate broadleaf trees. The forest around the shrine consists of evergreen broadleaf species dominated by {{ill|Castanopsis cuspidata|ja|コジイ}} and {{ill|Elaeocarpus japonicus|ja|ミミズバイ}}, along with {{ill|Holtunok|ja|ホルトノキ}}, {{ill|Ardisia japonica|ja|タイミンタチバナ}}, and {{ill|Corchoropsis tomentosa|ja|ツルコウジ}}. This area was designated a Natural Monument by Kishiwada City in 1968 (Shōwa 43). It is also listed among the {{ill|Osaka 100 Best Green Sites|ja|大阪みどりの百選}} and was selected as an Osaka Prefecture Natural Conservation Area in 1989 (Heisei 1). However, the natural environment around the shrine, including Kōnoyama, has suffered significant degradation over the past 50 years. Restoration efforts are ongoing under the "Kōnoyama Area Natural Restoration Comprehensive Plan" (October 2004) and the "Kōnoyama Area Natural Restoration Project Implementation Plan" (June 2005), supervised by the Ministry of Agriculture, Forestry and Fisheries. ## Access - **By Bus**: From {{ill|Kishiwada Station|ja|岸和田駅}} (Nankai Electric Railway) or {{ill|Higashi-Kishiwada Station|ja|東岸和田駅}} (JR Hanwa Line), take the Nankai Wing Bus South on routes 0, 41, 42, or 43 to Miyano-dai. Buses run approximately twice per hour. - **By Car**: Use {{ill|Hanwa Expressway|ja|阪和自動車道}}, exiting at either Kaizuka IC or Kishiwada-Izumi IC, and follow National Route 170. ## References {{Reflist}} {{Commons|Category:Ogami-jinja, Kishiwada}} {{Osaka 100 Best Green Sites}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Ogami Shrine}} [[Category:Shikinaisha in Izumi Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Kishiwada]] [[Category:Buildings in Kishiwada]] [[Category:Former village shrines in Japan]] [[Category:Https://ja.wikipedia.org/w/index.php?title=]][[Category:unsorted link categories]][[category:unsorted completion categories]][[ja:%E6%84%8F%E8%B3%80%E7%BE%8E%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE (%E5%B2%B8%E5%92%8C%E7%94%B0%E5%B8%82)]][[Category:unsorted link categories]][[category:unsorted completion categories]][[ja:&action=edit]] [[Category:unsorted link categories]][[category:unsorted completion categories]][[ja:意賀美神社]]

Buses run approximately twice per hour. - **By Car**: Use {{ill|Hanwa Expressway|ja|阪和自動車道}}, exiting at either Kaizuka IC or Kishiwada-Izumi IC, and follow National Route 170. == References == {{Reflist}} {{Commons|Category:Ogami-jinja, Kishiwada}} {{Osaka 100 Best Green Sites}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Ogami Shrine}} [[Category:Shikinaisha in Izumi Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Kishiwada]] [[Category:Buildings in Kishiwada]] [[Category:Former village shrines in Japan]] [[Category:Https://ja.wikipedia.org/w/index.php?title=]][[Category:unsorted link categories]][[category:unsorted completion categories]][[ja:%E6%84%8F%E8%B3%80%E7%BE%8E%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE (%E5%B2%B8%E5%92%8C%E7%94%B0%E5%B8%82)]][[Category:unsorted link categories]][[category:unsorted completion categories]][[ja:&action=edit]] [[Category:unsorted link categories]][[category:unsorted completion categories]][[ja:意賀美神社]]

The forest around the shrine consists of evergreen broadleaf species dominated by {{ill|Castanopsis cuspidata|ja|コジイ}} and {{ill|Elaeocarpus japonicus|ja|ミミズバイ}}, along with {{ill|Holtunok|ja|ホルトノキ}}, {{ill|Ardisia japonica|ja|タイミンタチバナ}}, and {{ill|Corchoropsis tomentosa|ja|ツルコウジ}}. This area was designated a Natural Monument by Kishiwada City in 1968 (Shōwa 43). It is also listed among the {{ill|Osaka 100 Best Green Sites|ja|大阪みどりの百選}} and was selected as an Osaka Prefecture Natural Conservation Area in 1989 (Heisei 1). However, the natural environment around the shrine, including Kōnoyama, has suffered significant degradation over the past 50 years. Restoration efforts are ongoing under the "Kōnoyama Area Natural Restoration Comprehensive Plan" (October 2004) and the "Kōnoyama Area Natural Restoration Project Implementation Plan" (June 2005), supervised by the Ministry of Agriculture, Forestry and Fisheries. == Access == - **By Bus**: From {{ill|Kishiwada Station|ja|岸和田駅}} (Nankai Electric Railway) or {{ill|Higashi-Kishiwada Station|ja|東岸和田駅}} (JR Hanwa Line), take the Nankai Wing Bus South on routes 0, 41, 42, or 43 to Miyano-dai. Buses run approximately twice per hour. - **By Car**: Use {{ill|Hanwa Expressway|ja|阪和自動車道}}, exiting at either Kaizuka IC or Kishiwada-Izumi IC, and follow National Route 170. == References == {{Reflist}} {{Commons|Category:Ogami-jinja, Kishiwada}} {{Osaka 100 Best Green Sites}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Ogami Shrine}} [[Category:Shikinaisha in Izumi Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Kishiwada]] [[Category:Buildings in Kishiwada]] [[Category:Former village shrines in Japan]] [[Category:Https://ja.wikipedia.org/w/index.php?title=]][[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[category:unsorted completion categories]][[ja:%E6%84%8F%E8%B3%80%E7%BE%8E%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE (%E5%B2%B8%E5%92%8C%E7%94%B0%E5%B8%82)]][[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[category:unsorted completion categories]][[ja:&action=edit]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Images]] [[Category:bump 1-20]] [[ja:意賀美神社]]

It is a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}} (Minor Shrine) and was classified as a village shrine under the {{ill|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|ja|近代社格制度}}. The shrine serves as the tutelary deity for the former Amakawataki and Habutaki villages and is located at an important water management site. == History == During the reign of {{ill|Emperor Shōmu|ja|聖武天皇}}, the shrine gained recognition in 732 CE (Tenpyō 4) for its efficacy in bringing rain during a severe drought. Similarly, during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Yōzei|ja|陽成天皇}} in 884 CE (Gangyō 8), {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|菅原道真}} offered prayers during another drought, and the shrine was revered for its rain-bringing miracles. Since then, the shrine has also been called the "Rainfall Deity Shrine." On August 14, 1906 (Meiji 39), under the {{ill|Shrine Consolidation Policy|ja|神社合祀}} directive, the {{ill|Furumoto Shrine|ja|古元神社}} and {{ill|Ichinose Shrine|ja|一瀬神社}}, both located in Habutaki, were merged with Ogami Shrine on November 11, 1906. == Cultural Assets == * Shrine plaque inscribed by {{ill|Kazanin Atsunaga|ja|花山院厚卿}} == Auxiliary Shrines == * Itsukushima Shrine * Ogami Kojin Shrine == Shrine Surroundings == The shrine is located at the northern foothills of {{ill|Kōnoyama|ja|神於山}}. The site includes the "Rainfall Waterfall" (雨降りの滝), which flows into a tributary of the Tsudagawa River. Due to its inland location, the region often experiences reduced rainfall after the rainy season, similar to the Seto Inland Sea climate. However, the natural environment around the shrine, including Kōnoyama, has suffered significant degradation over the past 50 years. On August 14, 1906 (Meiji 39), under the {{ill|Shrine Consolidation Policy|ja|神社合祀}} directive, the {{ill|Furumoto Shrine|ja|古元神社}} and {{ill|Ichinose Shrine|ja|一瀬神社}}, both located in Habutaki, were merged with Ogami Shrine on November 11, 1906. == Cultural Assets == * Shrine plaque inscribed by {{ill|Kazanin Atsunaga|ja|花山院厚卿}} == Auxiliary Shrines == * Itsukushima Shrine * Ogami Kojin Shrine == Shrine Surroundings == The shrine is located at the northern foothills of {{ill|Kōnoyama|ja|神於山}}. The site includes the "Rainfall Waterfall" (雨降りの滝), which flows into a tributary of the Tsudagawa River. Due to its inland location, the region often experiences reduced rainfall after the rainy season, similar to the Seto Inland Sea climate. However, the shrine's surroundings are notably humid, supporting diverse vegetation, including rare warm-temperate broadleaf trees. The forest around the shrine consists of evergreen broadleaf species dominated by {{ill|Castanopsis cuspidata|ja|コジイ}} and {{ill|Elaeocarpus japonicus|ja|ミミズバイ}}, along with {{ill|Holtunok|ja|ホルトノキ}}, {{ill|Ardisia japonica|ja|タイミンタチバナ}}, and {{ill|Corchoropsis tomentosa|ja|ツルコウジ}}. This area was designated a Natural Monument by Kishiwada City in 1968 (Shōwa 43). It is also listed among the {{ill|Osaka 100 Best Green Sites|ja|大阪みどりの百選}} and was selected as an Osaka Prefecture Natural Conservation Area in 1989 (Heisei 1). However, the natural environment around the shrine, including Kōnoyama, has suffered significant degradation over the past 50 years. Restoration efforts are ongoing under the "Kōnoyama Area Natural Restoration Comprehensive Plan" (October 2004) and the "Kōnoyama Area Natural Restoration Project Implementation Plan" (June 2005), supervised by the Ministry of Agriculture, Forestry and Fisheries. == Access == - **By Bus**: From {{ill|Kishiwada Station|ja|岸和田駅}} (Nankai Electric Railway) or {{ill|Higashi-Kishiwada Station|ja|東岸和田駅}} (JR Hanwa Line), take the Nankai Wing Bus South on routes 0, 41, 42, or 43 to Miyano-dai. Buses run approximately twice per hour. - **By Car**: Use {{ill|Hanwa Expressway|ja|阪和自動車道}}, exiting at either Kaizuka IC or Kishiwada-Izumi IC, and follow National Route 170. == References == {{Reflist}} {{Commons|Category:Ogami-jinja, Kishiwada}} {{Osaka 100 Best Green Sites}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Ogami Shrine}} [[Category:Shikinaisha in Izumi Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Kishiwada]] [[Category:Buildings in Kishiwada]] [[Category:Former village shrines in Japan]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[category:unsorted completion categories]][[ja:%E6%84%8F%E8%B3%80%E7%BE%8E%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE (%E5%B2%B8%E5%92%8C%E7%94%B0%E5%B8%82)]][[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[category:unsorted completion categories]][[ja:&action=edit]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:images]] [[Category:bump 1-27]] [[ja:意賀美神社]]

Ichikishimahime-no-Mikoto
Hachidai Ryūō and Gozu Tennō
Itaki Iwa-no-Kami | Shrine_rank = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}} (Minor), Former Village Shrine | Foundation = Before the {{ill|Tenpyō era|ja|天平}} (729–749) | Main_building = {{ill|Hafu-zukuri|ja|破風造}} | Festival = October 5 }} '''Ogami Shrine''' (意賀美神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Kishiwada|ja|岸和田市}}, Osaka Prefecture, Japan. It is a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}} (Minor Shrine) and was classified as a village shrine under the {{ill|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|ja|近代社格制度}}. The shrine serves as the tutelary deity for the former Amakawataki and Habutaki villages and is located at an important water management site. == History == During the reign of {{ill|Emperor Shōmu|ja|聖武天皇}}, the shrine gained recognition in 732 CE (Tenpyō 4) for its efficacy in bringing rain during a severe drought. Similarly, during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Yōzei|ja|陽成天皇}} in 884 CE (Gangyō 8), {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|菅原道真}} offered prayers during another drought, and the shrine was revered for its rain-bringing miracles. Since then, the shrine has also been called the "Rainfall Deity Shrine." On August 14, 1906 (Meiji 39), under the {{ill|Shrine Consolidation Policy|ja|神社合祀}} directive, the {{ill|Furumoto Shrine|ja|古元神社}} and {{ill|Ichinose Shrine|ja|一瀬神社}}, both located in Habutaki, were merged with Ogami Shrine on November 11, 1906. == Cultural Assets == * Shrine plaque inscribed by {{ill|Kazanin Atsunaga|ja|花山院厚卿}} == Auxiliary Shrines == * Itsukushima Shrine * Ogami Kojin Shrine == Shrine Surroundings == The shrine is located at the northern foothills of {{ill|Kōnoyama|ja|神於山}}. The site includes the "Rainfall Waterfall" (雨降りの滝), which flows into a tributary of the Tsudagawa River. Due to its inland location, the region often experiences reduced rainfall after the rainy season, similar to the Seto Inland Sea climate. However, the shrine's surroundings are notably humid, supporting diverse vegetation, including rare warm-temperate broadleaf trees. The forest around the shrine consists of evergreen broadleaf species dominated by {{ill|Castanopsis cuspidata|ja|コジイ}} and {{ill|Elaeocarpus japonicus|ja|ミミズバイ}}, along with {{ill|Holtunok|ja|ホルトノキ}}, {{ill|Ardisia japonica|ja|タイミンタチバナ}}, and {{ill|Corchoropsis tomentosa|ja|ツルコウジ}}. This area was designated a Natural Monument by Kishiwada City in 1968 (Shōwa 43). It is also listed among the {{ill|Osaka 100 Best Green Sites|ja|大阪みどりの百選}} and was selected as an Osaka Prefecture Natural Conservation Area in 1989 (Heisei 1). However, the natural environment around the shrine, including Kōnoyama, has suffered significant degradation over the past 50 years. Restoration efforts are ongoing under the "Kōnoyama Area Natural Restoration Comprehensive Plan" (October 2004) and the "Kōnoyama Area Natural Restoration Project Implementation Plan" (June 2005), supervised by the Ministry of Agriculture, Forestry and Fisheries. == Access == - **By Bus**: From {{ill|Kishiwada Station|ja|岸和田駅}} (Nankai Electric Railway) or {{ill|Higashi-Kishiwada Station|ja|東岸和田駅}} (JR Hanwa Line), take the Nankai Wing Bus South on routes 0, 41, 42, or 43 to Miyano-dai. Buses run approximately twice per hour. - **By Car**: Use {{ill|Hanwa Expressway|ja|阪和自動車道}}, exiting at either Kaizuka IC or Kishiwada-Izumi IC, and follow National Route 170. == References == {{Reflist}} {{Commons|Category:Ogami-jinja, Kishiwada}} {{Osaka 100 Best Green Sites}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Ogami Shrine}} [[Category:Shikinaisha in Izumi Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Kishiwada]] [[Category:Buildings in Kishiwada]] [[Category:Former village shrines in Japan]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]] [[Category:pages needing attribution]][[Category:Okami Shrine (Kishiwada)]][[Category:pages needing attribution]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:pages needing attribution]][[Category:Okami Shrine (Kishiwada)]][[Category:pages needing attribution]] [[ja:%E6%84%8F%E8%B3%80%E7%BE%8E%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE (%E5%B2%B8%E5%92%8C%E7%94%B0%E5%B8%82)]][[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]] [[Category:pages needing attribution]][[Category:Okami Shrine (Kishiwada)]][[Category:pages needing attribution]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:pages needing attribution]][[Category:Okami Shrine (Kishiwada)]][[Category:pages needing attribution]] [[ja:&action=edit]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]] [[Category:pages needing attribution]][[Category:Okami Shrine (Kishiwada)]][[Category:pages needing attribution]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:pages needing attribution]][[Category:Okami Shrine (Kishiwada)]][[Category:pages needing attribution]] [[Category:images]] [[Category:bump 1-27]] [[ja:意賀美神社]]

The forest around the shrine consists of evergreen broadleaf species dominated by {{ill|Castanopsis cuspidata|ja|コジイ}} and {{ill|Elaeocarpus japonicus|ja|ミミズバイ}}, along with {{ill|Holtunok|ja|ホルトノキ}}, {{ill|Ardisia japonica|ja|タイミンタチバナ}}, and {{ill|Corchoropsis tomentosa|ja|ツルコウジ}}. This area was designated a Natural Monument by Kishiwada City in 1968 (Shōwa 43). It is also listed among the {{ill|Osaka 100 Best Green Sites|ja|大阪みどりの百選}} and was selected as an Osaka Prefecture Natural Conservation Area in 1989 (Heisei 1). However, the natural environment around the shrine, including Kōnoyama, has suffered significant degradation over the past 50 years. Restoration efforts are ongoing under the "Kōnoyama Area Natural Restoration Comprehensive Plan" (October 2004) and the "Kōnoyama Area Natural Restoration Project Implementation Plan" (June 2005), supervised by the Ministry of Agriculture, Forestry and Fisheries. == Access == - **By Bus**: From {{ill|Kishiwada Station|ja|岸和田駅}} (Nankai Electric Railway) or {{ill|Higashi-Kishiwada Station|ja|東岸和田駅}} (JR Hanwa Line), take the Nankai Wing Bus South on routes 0, 41, 42, or 43 to Miyano-dai. Itaki Iwa-no-Kami | Shrine_rank = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}} (Minor), Former Village Shrine | Foundation = Before the {{ill|Tenpyō era|ja|天平}} (729–749) | Main_building = {{ill|Hafu-zukuri|ja|破風造}} | Festival = October 5 }} '''Ogami Shrine''' (意賀美神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Kishiwada|ja|岸和田市}}, Osaka Prefecture, Japan. It is a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}} (Minor Shrine) and was classified as a village shrine under the {{ill|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|ja|近代社格制度}}. The shrine serves as the tutelary deity for the former Amakawataki and Habutaki villages and is located at an important water management site. == History == During the reign of {{ill|Emperor Shōmu|ja|聖武天皇}}, the shrine gained recognition in 732 CE (Tenpyō 4) for its efficacy in bringing rain during a severe drought. Similarly, during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Yōzei|ja|陽成天皇}} in 884 CE (Gangyō 8), {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|菅原道真}} offered prayers during another drought, and the shrine was revered for its rain-bringing miracles. Since then, the shrine has also been called the "Rainfall deity Shrine." On August 14, 1906 (Meiji 39), under the {{ill|Shrine Consolidation Policy|ja|神社合祀}} directive, the {{ill|Furumoto Shrine|ja|古元神社}} and {{ill|Ichinose Shrine|ja|一瀬神社}}, both located in Habutaki, were merged with Ogami Shrine on November 11, 1906. == Cultural Assets == * Shrine plaque inscribed by {{ill|Kazanin Atsunaga|ja|花山院厚卿}} == Auxiliary Shrines == * Itsukushima Shrine * Ogami Kojin Shrine == Shrine Surroundings == The shrine is located at the northern foothills of {{ill|Kōnoyama|ja|神於山}}. The site includes the "Rainfall Waterfall" (雨降りの滝), which flows into a tributary of the Tsudagawa River. Due to its inland location, the region often experiences reduced rainfall after the rainy season, similar to the Seto Inland Sea climate. However, the shrine's surroundings are notably humid, supporting diverse vegetation, including rare warm-temperate broadleaf trees. The forest around the shrine consists of evergreen broadleaf species dominated by {{ill|Castanopsis cuspidata|ja|コジイ}} and {{ill|Elaeocarpus japonicus|ja|ミミズバイ}}, along with {{ill|Holtunok|ja|ホルトノキ}}, {{ill|Ardisia japonica|ja|タイミンタチバナ}}, and {{ill|Corchoropsis tomentosa|ja|ツルコウジ}}. It is also listed among the {{ill|Osaka 100 Best Green Sites|ja|大阪みどりの百選}} and was selected as an Osaka Prefecture Natural Conservation Area in 1989 (Heisei 1). However, the natural environment around the shrine, including Kōnoyama, has suffered significant degradation over the past 50 years. Restoration efforts are ongoing under the "Kōnoyama Area Natural Restoration Comprehensive Plan" (October 2004) and the "Kōnoyama Area Natural Restoration Project Implementation Plan" (June 2005), supervised by the Ministry of Agriculture, Forestry and Fisheries. == Access == - **By Bus**: From {{ill|Kishiwada Station|ja|岸和田駅}} (Nankai Electric Railway) or {{ill|Higashi-Kishiwada Station|ja|東岸和田駅}} (JR Hanwa Line), take the Nankai Wing Bus South on routes 0, 41, 42, or 43 to Miyano-dai. Buses run approximately twice per hour. - **By Car**: Use {{ill|Hanwa Expressway|ja|阪和自動車道}}, exiting at either Kaizuka IC or Kishiwada-Izumi IC, and follow National Route 170. == References == {{Reflist}} {{Commons|Category:Ogami-jinja, Kishiwada}} {{Osaka 100 Best Green Sites}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Ogami Shrine}} [[Category:Shikinaisha in Izumi Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Kishiwada]] [[Category:Buildings in Kishiwada]] [[Category:Former village shrines in Japan]] [[Category:Okami Shrine (Kishiwada)]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq-{{subst:MAINSPACE}}--qq]] [[Category:Okami Shrine (Kishiwada)]][[ja:%E6%84%8F%E8%B3%80%E7%BE%8E%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE (%E5%B2%B8%E5%92%8C%E7%94%B0%E5%B8%82)]][[Category:Okami Shrine (Kishiwada)]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq-{{subst:MAINSPACE}}--qq]] [[Category:Okami Shrine (Kishiwada)]][[ja:&action=edit]] [[Category:Okami Shrine (Kishiwada)]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq-{{subst:MAINSPACE}}--qq]] [[Category:Okami Shrine (Kishiwada)]][[Category:images]] [[Category:bump 1-27]] [[ja:意賀美神社]]

Buses run approximately twice per hour. - **By Car**: Use {{ill|Hanwa Expressway|ja|阪和自動車道}}, exiting at either Kaizuka IC or Kishiwada-Izumi IC, and follow National Route 170. == References == {{Reflist}} {{Commons|Category:Ogami-jinja, Kishiwada}} {{Osaka 100 Best Green Sites}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Ogami Shrine}} [[Category:Shikinaisha in Izumi Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Kishiwada]] [[Category:Buildings in Kishiwada]] [[Category:Former village shrines in Japan]] [[Category:Okami Shrine (Kishiwada)]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:Okami Shrine (Kishiwada)]][[ja:%E6%84%8F%E8%B3%80%E7%BE%8E%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE (%E5%B2%B8%E5%92%8C%E7%94%B0%E5%B8%82)]][[Category:Okami Shrine (Kishiwada)]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:Okami Shrine (Kishiwada)]][[ja:&action=edit]] [[Category:Okami Shrine (Kishiwada)]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:Okami Shrine (Kishiwada)]][[Category:images]] [[Category:bump 1-27]] [[sn:Okami Shrine (Kishiwada)]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Okami Shrine (Kishiwada)]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Okami Shrine (Kishiwada)]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Okami Shrine (Kishiwada)]][[Category:qq]] [[ja:意賀美神社]]

{{Shrine | name = Ogami Shrine (意賀美神社) | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:OgamiJinjaHaiden.jpg|250px|意賀美神社拝殿]]
Haiden {{Maplink2|zoom=12|frame=yes|plain=yes|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|frame-height=220|type=point|frame-latitude=34.437|frame-longitude=135.40|coord={{coord|34|24|57.54|N|135|24|38.94|E}}}} | location = 17 Habutaki-cho, Kishiwada City, Osaka Prefecture, Japan | coordinates = {{Coord|34|24|57.54|N|135|24|38.94|E|region:JP-27_scale:30000|display=inline,title}} | Deities = Yamigami-no-Ōkami
Ichikishimahime-no-Mikoto
Hachidai Ryūō and Gozu Tennō
Itaki Iwa-no-Kami | Shrine_rank = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}} (Minor), Former Village Shrine | Foundation = Before the {{ill|Tenpyō era|ja|天平}} (729–749) | Main_building = {{ill|Hafu-zukuri|ja|破風造}} | Festival = October 5 }} '''Ogami Shrine''' (意賀美神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Kishiwada|ja|岸和田市}}, Osaka Prefecture, Japan. It is a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}} (Minor Shrine) and was classified as a village shrine under the {{ill|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|ja|近代社格制度}}. The shrine serves as the tutelary deity for the former Amakawataki and Habutaki villages and is located at an important water management site. == History == During the reign of {{ill|Emperor Shōmu|ja|聖武天皇}}, the shrine gained recognition in 732 CE (Tenpyō 4) for its efficacy in bringing rain during a severe drought. Similarly, during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Yōzei|ja|陽成天皇}} in 884 CE (Gangyō 8), {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|菅原道真}} offered prayers during another drought, and the shrine was revered for its rain-bringing miracles. Since then, the shrine has also been called the "Rainfall deity Shrine." On August 14, 1906 (Meiji 39), under the {{ill|Shrine Consolidation Policy|ja|神社合祀}} directive, the {{ill|Furumoto Shrine|ja|古元神社}} and {{ill|Ichinose Shrine|ja|一瀬神社}}, both located in Habutaki, were merged with Ogami Shrine on November 11, 1906. == Cultural Assets == * Shrine plaque inscribed by {{ill|Kazanin Atsunaga|ja|花山院厚卿}} == Auxiliary Shrines == * Itsukushima Shrine * Ogami Kojin Shrine == Shrine Surroundings == The shrine is located at the northern foothills of {{ill|Kōnoyama|ja|神於山}}. The site includes the "Rainfall Waterfall" (雨降りの滝), which flows into a tributary of the Tsudagawa River. Due to its inland location, the region often experiences reduced rainfall after the rainy season, similar to the Seto Inland Sea climate. However, the shrine's surroundings are notably humid, supporting diverse vegetation, including rare warm-temperate broadleaf trees. The forest around the shrine consists of evergreen broadleaf species dominated by {{ill|Castanopsis cuspidata|ja|コジイ}} and {{ill|Elaeocarpus japonicus|ja|ミミズバイ}}, along with {{ill|Holtunok|ja|ホルトノキ}}, {{ill|Ardisia japonica|ja|タイミンタチバナ}}, and {{ill|Corchoropsis tomentosa|ja|ツルコウジ}}. This area was designated a Natural Monument by Kishiwada City in 1968 (Shōwa 43). It is also listed among the {{ill|Osaka 100 Best Green Sites|ja|大阪みどりの百選}} and was selected as an Osaka Prefecture Natural Conservation Area in 1989 (Heisei 1). However, the natural environment around the shrine, including Kōnoyama, has suffered significant degradation over the past 50 years. Restoration efforts are ongoing under the "Kōnoyama Area Natural Restoration Comprehensive Plan" (October 2004) and the "Kōnoyama Area Natural Restoration Project Implementation Plan" (June 2005), supervised by the Ministry of Agriculture, Forestry and Fisheries. == Access == - **By Bus**: From {{ill|Kishiwada Station|ja|岸和田駅}} (Nankai Electric Railway) or {{ill|Higashi-Kishiwada Station|ja|東岸和田駅}} (JR Hanwa Line), take the Nankai Wing Bus South on routes 0, 41, 42, or 43 to Miyano-dai. Buses run approximately twice per hour. - **By Car**: Use {{ill|Hanwa Expressway|ja|阪和自動車道}}, exiting at either Kaizuka IC or Kishiwada-Izumi IC, and follow National Route 170. == References == {{Reflist}} {{Commons|Category:Ogami-jinja, Kishiwada}} {{Osaka 100 Best Green Sites}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Ogami Shrine}} [[Category:Shikinaisha in Izumi Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Kishiwada]] [[Category:Buildings in Kishiwada]] [[Category:Former village shrines in Japan]] [[Category:Okami Shrine (Kishiwada)]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Okami Shrine (Kishiwada)]][[ja:%E6%84%8F%E8%B3%80%E7%BE%8E%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE (%E5%B2%B8%E5%92%8C%E7%94%B0%E5%B8%82)]][[Category:Okami Shrine (Kishiwada)]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Okami Shrine (Kishiwada)]][[ja:&action=edit]] [[Category:Okami Shrine (Kishiwada)]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Okami Shrine (Kishiwada)]][[Category:images]] [[Category:bump 1-27]] [[sn:Okami Shrine (Kishiwada)]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Okami Shrine (Kishiwada)]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Okami Shrine (Kishiwada)]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Okami Shrine (Kishiwada)]][[Category:qq]] [[ja:意賀美神社]]

Itaki Iwa-no-Kami | Shrine_rank = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}} (Minor), Former Village Shrine | Foundation = Before the {{ill|Tenpyō era|ja|天平}} (729–749) | Main_building = {{ill|Hafu-zukuri|ja|破風造}} | Festival = October 5 }} '''Ogami Shrine''' (意賀美神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Kishiwada|ja|岸和田市}}, Osaka Prefecture, Japan. It is a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}} (Minor Shrine) and was classified as a village shrine under the {{ill|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|ja|近代社格制度}}. The shrine serves as the tutelary deity for the former Amakawataki and Habutaki villages and is located at an important water management site. == History == During the reign of {{ill|Emperor Shōmu|ja|聖武天皇}}, the shrine gained recognition in 732 CE (Tenpyō 4) for its efficacy in bringing rain during a severe drought. Similarly, during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Yōzei|ja|陽成天皇}} in 884 CE (Gangyō 8), {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|菅原道真}} offered prayers during another drought, and the shrine was revered for its rain-bringing miracles. Since then, the shrine has also been called the "Rainfall deity Shrine." On August 14, 1906 (Meiji 39), under the {{ill|Shrine Consolidation Policy|ja|神社合祀|1=Shrine Consolidation Policy}} directive, the {{ill|Furumoto Shrine|ja|古元神社}} and {{ill|Ichinose Shrine|ja|一瀬神社}}, both located in Habutaki, were merged with Ogami Shrine on November 11, 1906. == Cultural Assets == * Shrine plaque inscribed by {{ill|Kazanin Atsunaga|ja|花山院厚卿}} == Auxiliary Shrines == * Itsukushima Shrine * Ogami Kojin Shrine == Shrine Surroundings == The shrine is located at the northern foothills of {{ill|Kōnoyama|ja|神於山}}. The site includes the "Rainfall Waterfall" (雨降りの滝), which flows into a tributary of the Tsudagawa River. Due to its inland location, the region often experiences reduced rainfall after the rainy season, similar to the Seto Inland Sea climate. However, the shrine's surroundings are notably humid, supporting diverse vegetation, including rare warm-temperate broadleaf trees. The forest around the shrine consists of evergreen broadleaf species dominated by {{ill|Castanopsis cuspidata|ja|コジイ}} and {{ill|Elaeocarpus japonicus|ja|ミミズバイ}}, along with {{ill|Holtunok|ja|ホルトノキ}}, {{ill|Ardisia japonica|ja|タイミンタチバナ}}, and {{ill|Corchoropsis tomentosa|ja|ツルコウジ}}. This area was designated a Natural Monument by Kishiwada City in 1968 (Shōwa 43). It is also listed among the {{ill|Osaka 100 Best Green Sites|ja|大阪みどりの百選}} and was selected as an Osaka Prefecture Natural Conservation Area in 1989 (Heisei 1). However, the natural environment around the shrine, including Kōnoyama, has suffered significant degradation over the past 50 years. Restoration efforts are ongoing under the "Kōnoyama Area Natural Restoration Comprehensive Plan" (October 2004) and the "Kōnoyama Area Natural Restoration Project Implementation Plan" (June 2005), supervised by the Ministry of Agriculture, Forestry and Fisheries. == Access == - **By Bus**: From {{ill|Kishiwada Station|ja|岸和田駅}} (Nankai Electric Railway) or {{ill|Higashi-Kishiwada Station|ja|東岸和田駅}} (JR Hanwa Line), take the Nankai Wing Bus South on routes 0, 41, 42, or 43 to Miyano-dai. Buses run approximately twice per hour. - **By Car**: Use {{ill|Hanwa Expressway|ja|阪和自動車道}}, exiting at either Kaizuka IC or Kishiwada-Izumi IC, and follow National Route 170. == References == {{Reflist}} {{Commons|Category:Ogami-jinja, Kishiwada}} {{Osaka 100 Best Green Sites}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Ogami Shrine}} [[Category:Shikinaisha in Izumi Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Kishiwada]] [[Category:Buildings in Kishiwada]] [[Category:Former village shrines in Japan]] [[Category:Okami Shrine (Kishiwada)]] [[Category:Completion tier 5]] [[Category:Okami Shrine (Kishiwada)]][[ja:%E6%84%8F%E8%B3%80%E7%BE%8E%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE (%E5%B2%B8%E5%92%8C%E7%94%B0%E5%B8%82)]][[Category:Okami Shrine (Kishiwada)]] [[Category:Okami Shrine (Kishiwada)]][[ja:&action=edit]] [[Category:Okami Shrine (Kishiwada)]] [[Category:Okami Shrine (Kishiwada)]][[Category:images]] [[Category:bump 1-27]] [[sn:Okami Shrine (Kishiwada)]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Okami Shrine (Kishiwada)]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Okami Shrine (Kishiwada)]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Okami Shrine (Kishiwada)]][[Category:qq]] [[ja:意賀美神社]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:New]]

However, the natural environment around the shrine, including Kōnoyama, has suffered significant degradation over the past 50 years. Restoration efforts are ongoing under the "Kōnoyama Area Natural Restoration Comprehensive Plan" (October 2004) and the "Kōnoyama Area Natural Restoration Project Implementation Plan" (June 2005), supervised by the Ministry of Agriculture, Forestry and Fisheries. == Access == - **By Bus**: From {{ill|Kishiwada Station|ja|岸和田駅}} (Nankai Electric Railway) or {{ill|Higashi-Kishiwada Station|ja|東岸和田駅}} (JR Hanwa Line), take the Nankai Wing Bus South on routes 0, 41, 42, or 43 to Miyano-dai. Buses run approximately twice per hour. - **By Car**: Use {{ill|Hanwa Expressway|ja|阪和自動車道}}, exiting at either Kaizuka IC or Kishiwada-Izumi IC, and follow National Route 170. == References == {{Reflist}} {{Commons|Category:Ogami-jinja, Kishiwada}} {{Osaka 100 Best Green Sites}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Ogami Shrine}} [[Category:Shikinaisha in Izumi Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Kishiwada]] [[Category:Buildings in Kishiwada]] [[Category:Former village shrines in Japan]] [[Category:Okami Shrine (Kishiwada)]] [[Category:Completion tier 5]] [[Category:Okami Shrine (Kishiwada)]][[ja:%E6%84%8F%E8%B3%80%E7%BE%8E%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE (%E5%B2%B8%E5%92%8C%E7%94%B0%E5%B8%82)]][[Category:Okami Shrine (Kishiwada)]] [[Category:Okami Shrine (Kishiwada)]][[ja:&action=edit]] [[Category:Okami Shrine (Kishiwada)]] [[Category:Okami Shrine (Kishiwada)]][[Category:bump 1-27]] [[sn:Okami Shrine (Kishiwada)]][[Category:11]][[sn:Okami Shrine (Kishiwada)]][[Category:11]][[sn:Okami Shrine (Kishiwada)]][[Category:11]][[sn:Okami Shrine (Kishiwada)]][[Category:11]] [[ja:意賀美神社]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:New]]

{{Shrine | name = Ogami Shrine (意賀美神社) | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:OgamiJinjaHaiden.jpg|250px|意賀美神社拝殿]]
Haiden {{Maplink2|zoom=12|frame=yes|plain=yes|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|frame-height=220|type=point|frame-latitude=34.437|frame-longitude=135.40|coord={{coord|34|24|57.54|N|135|24|38.94|E}}}} | location = 17 Habutaki-cho, Kishiwada City, Osaka Prefecture, Japan | coordinates = {{Coord|34|24|57.54|N|135|24|38.94|E|region:JP-27_scale:30000|display=inline,title}} | Deities = Yamigami-no-Ōkami
Ichikishimahime-no-Mikoto
Hachidai Ryūō and Gozu Tennō
Itaki Iwa-no-Kami | Shrine_rank = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}} (Minor), Former Village Shrine | Foundation = Before the {{ill|Tenpyō era|ja|天平}} (729–749) | Main_building = {{ill|Hafu-zukuri|ja|破風造}} | Festival = October 5 }} '''Ogami Shrine''' (意賀美神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Kishiwada|ja|岸和田市}}, Osaka Prefecture, Japan. It is a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}} (Minor Shrine) and was classified as a village shrine under the {{ill|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|ja|近代社格制度|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}}. The shrine serves as the tutelary deity for the former Amakawataki and Habutaki villages and is located at an important water management site. == History == During the reign of {{ill|Emperor Shōmu|ja|聖武天皇}}, the shrine gained recognition in 732 CE (Tenpyō 4) for its efficacy in bringing rain during a severe drought. Similarly, during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Yōzei|ja|陽成天皇}} in 884 CE (Gangyō 8), {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|Sugawara no Michizane}} offered prayers during another drought, and the shrine was revered for its rain-bringing miracles. Since then, the shrine has also been called the "Rainfall deity Shrine." On August 14, 1906 (Meiji 39), under the {{ill|Shrine Consolidation Policy|ja|神社合祀|1=Shrine Consolidation Policy}} directive, the {{ill|Furumoto Shrine|ja|古元神社}} and {{ill|Ichinose Shrine|ja|一瀬神社}}, both located in Habutaki, were merged with Ogami Shrine on November 11, 1906. == Cultural Assets == * Shrine plaque inscribed by {{ill|Kazanin Atsunaga|ja|花山院厚卿}} == Auxiliary Shrines == * Itsukushima Shrine * Ogami Kojin Shrine == Shrine Surroundings == The shrine is located at the northern foothills of {{ill|Kōnoyama|ja|神於山}}. The site includes the "Rainfall Waterfall" (雨降りの滝), which flows into a tributary of the Tsudagawa River. Due to its inland location, the region often experiences reduced rainfall after the rainy season, similar to the Seto Inland Sea climate. However, the shrine's surroundings are notably humid, supporting diverse vegetation, including rare warm-temperate broadleaf trees. The forest around the shrine consists of evergreen broadleaf species dominated by {{ill|Castanopsis cuspidata|ja|コジイ}} and {{ill|Elaeocarpus japonicus|ja|ミミズバイ}}, along with {{ill|Holtunok|ja|ホルトノキ}}, {{ill|Ardisia japonica|ja|タイミンタチバナ}}, and {{ill|Corchoropsis tomentosa|ja|ツルコウジ}}. This area was designated a Natural Monument by Kishiwada City in 1968 (Shōwa 43). It is also listed among the {{ill|Osaka 100 Best Green Sites|ja|大阪みどりの百選}} and was selected as an Osaka Prefecture Natural Conservation Area in 1989 (Heisei 1). However, the natural environment around the shrine, including Kōnoyama, has suffered significant degradation over the past 50 years. Restoration efforts are ongoing under the "Kōnoyama Area Natural Restoration Comprehensive Plan" (October 2004) and the "Kōnoyama Area Natural Restoration Project Implementation Plan" (June 2005), supervised by the Ministry of Agriculture, Forestry and Fisheries. == Access == - **By Bus**: From {{ill|Kishiwada Station|ja|岸和田駅}} (Nankai Electric Railway) or {{ill|Higashi-Kishiwada Station|ja|東岸和田駅}} (JR Hanwa Line), take the Nankai Wing Bus South on routes 0, 41, 42, or 43 to Miyano-dai. Buses run approximately twice per hour. - **By Car**: Use {{ill|Hanwa Expressway|ja|阪和自動車道}}, exiting at either Kaizuka IC or Kishiwada-Izumi IC, and follow National Route 170. == References == {{Reflist}} {{Commons|Category:Ogami-jinja, Kishiwada}} {{Osaka 100 Best Green Sites}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Ogami Shrine}} [[Category:Shikinaisha in Izumi Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Kishiwada]] [[Category:Buildings in Kishiwada]] [[Category:Former village shrines in Japan]] [[Category:Okami Shrine (Kishiwada)]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:Okami Shrine (Kishiwada)]][[ja:%E6%84%8F%E8%B3%80%E7%BE%8E%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE (%E5%B2%B8%E5%92%8C%E7%94%B0%E5%B8%82)]][[Category:Okami Shrine (Kishiwada)]] [[Category:Okami Shrine (Kishiwada)]][[ja:&action=edit]] [[Category:Okami Shrine (Kishiwada)]] [[Category:Okami Shrine (Kishiwada)]][[Category:bump 1-27]] [[sn:Okami Shrine (Kishiwada)]][[Category:11]][[sn:Okami Shrine (Kishiwada)]][[Category:11]][[sn:Okami Shrine (Kishiwada)]][[Category:11]][[sn:Okami Shrine (Kishiwada)]][[Category:11]] [[ja:意賀美神社]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:New]]

{{Shrine | name = Ogami Shrine (意賀美神社) | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:OgamiJinjaHaiden.jpg|250px|意賀美神社拝殿]]
Haiden {{Maplink2|zoom=12|frame=yes|plain=yes|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|frame-height=220|type=point|frame-latitude=34.437|frame-longitude=135.40|coord={{coord|34|24|57.54|N|135|24|38.94|E}}}} | location = 17 Habutaki-cho, Kishiwada City, Osaka Prefecture, Japan | coordinates = {{Coord|34|24|57.54|N|135|24|38.94|E|region:JP-27_scale:30000|display=inline,title}} | Deities = Yamigami-no-Ōkami
Ichikishimahime-no-Mikoto
Hachidai Ryūō and Gozu Tennō
Itaki Iwa-no-Kami | Shrine_rank = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}} (Minor), Former Village Shrine | Foundation = Before the {{ill|Tenpyō era|ja|天平}} (729–749) | Main_building = {{ill|Hafu-zukuri|ja|破風造}} | Festival = October 5 }} '''Ogami Shrine''' (意賀美神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Kishiwada|ja|岸和田市}}, Osaka Prefecture, Japan. It is a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}} (Minor Shrine) and was classified as a village shrine under the {{ill|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|ja|近代社格制度|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}}. The shrine serves as the tutelary deity for the former Amakawataki and Habutaki villages and is located at an important water management site. == History == During the reign of {{ill|Emperor Shōmu|ja|聖武天皇}}, the shrine gained recognition in 732 CE (Tenpyō 4) for its efficacy in bringing rain during a severe drought. Similarly, during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Yōzei|ja|陽成天皇}} in 884 CE (Gangyō 8), {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|Sugawara no Michizane}} offered prayers during another drought, and the shrine was revered for its rain-bringing miracles. Since then, the shrine has also been called the "Rainfall deity Shrine." On August 14, 1906 (Meiji 39), under the {{ill|Shrine Consolidation Policy|ja|神社合祀|1=Shrine Consolidation Policy}} directive, the {{ill|Furumoto Shrine|ja|古元神社}} and {{ill|Ichinose Shrine|ja|一瀬神社}}, both located in Habutaki, were merged with Ogami Shrine on November 11, 1906. == Cultural Assets == * Shrine plaque inscribed by {{ill|Kazanin Atsunaga|ja|花山院厚卿}} == Auxiliary Shrines == * Itsukushima Shrine * Ogami Kojin Shrine == Shrine Surroundings == The shrine is located at the northern foothills of {{ill|Kōnoyama|ja|神於山}}. The site includes the "Rainfall Waterfall" (雨降りの滝), which flows into a tributary of the Tsudagawa River. Due to its inland location, the region often experiences reduced rainfall after the rainy season, similar to the Seto Inland Sea climate. However, the shrine's surroundings are notably humid, supporting diverse vegetation, including rare warm-temperate broadleaf trees. The forest around the shrine consists of evergreen broadleaf species dominated by {{ill|Castanopsis cuspidata|ja|コジイ}} and {{ill|Elaeocarpus japonicus|ja|ミミズバイ}}, along with {{ill|Holtunok|ja|ホルトノキ}}, {{ill|Ardisia japonica|ja|タイミンタチバナ}}, and {{ill|Corchoropsis tomentosa|ja|ツルコウジ}}. This area was designated a Natural Monument by Kishiwada City in 1968 (Shōwa 43). It is also listed among the {{ill|Osaka 100 Best Green Sites|ja|大阪みどりの百選}} and was selected as an Osaka Prefecture Natural Conservation Area in 1989 (Heisei 1). However, the natural environment around the shrine, including Kōnoyama, has suffered significant degradation over the past 50 years. Restoration efforts are ongoing under the "Kōnoyama Area Natural Restoration Comprehensive Plan" (October 2004) and the "Kōnoyama Area Natural Restoration Project Implementation Plan" (June 2005), supervised by the Ministry of Agriculture, Forestry and Fisheries. == Access == - **By Bus**: From {{ill|Kishiwada Station|ja|岸和田駅}} (Nankai Electric Railway) or {{ill|Higashi-Kishiwada Station|ja|東岸和田駅}} (JR Hanwa Line), take the Nankai Wing Bus South on routes 0, 41, 42, or 43 to Miyano-dai. Buses run approximately twice per hour. - **By Car**: Use {{ill|Hanwa Expressway|ja|阪和自動車道}}, exiting at either Kaizuka IC or Kishiwada-Izumi IC, and follow National Route 170. == References == {{Reflist}} {{Commons|Category:Ogami-jinja, Kishiwada}} {{Osaka 100 Best Green Sites}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Ogami Shrine}} [[Category:Shikinaisha in Izumi Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Kishiwada]] [[Category:Buildings in Kishiwada]] [[Category:Former village shrines in Japan]] [[Category:Okami Shrine (Kishiwada)]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]][[Category: tier 6]][[Category: tier 6]] [[Category:Okami Shrine (Kishiwada)]][[ja:%E6%84%8F%E8%B3%80%E7%BE%8E%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE (%E5%B2%B8%E5%92%8C%E7%94%B0%E5%B8%82)]][[Category:Okami Shrine (Kishiwada)]] [[Category:Okami Shrine (Kishiwada)]][[ja:&action=edit]] [[Category:Okami Shrine (Kishiwada)]] [[Category:Okami Shrine (Kishiwada)]][[sn:Okami Shrine (Kishiwada)]][[Category:11]][[sn:Okami Shrine (Kishiwada)]][[Category:11]][[sn:Okami Shrine (Kishiwada)]][[Category:11]][[sn:Okami Shrine (Kishiwada)]][[Category:11]] [[ja:意賀美神社]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Okami Shrine (Kishiwada)]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:New]]

Itaki Iwa-no-Kami | Shrine_rank = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|12=draft}} (Minor), Former Village Shrine | Foundation = Before the {{ill|Tenpyō era|ja|天平|12=draft}} (729–749) | Main_building = {{ill|Hafu-zukuri|ja|破風造|12=draft}} | Festival = October 5 }} '''Ogami Shrine''' (意賀美神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社|12=draft}} located in {{ill|Kishiwada|ja|岸和田市|12=draft}}, Osaka Prefecture, Japan. It is a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|12=draft}} (Minor Shrine) and was classified as a village shrine under the {{ill|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|ja|近代社格制度|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=draft}}. The shrine serves as the tutelary deity for the former Amakawataki and Habutaki villages and is located at an important water management site. == History == During the reign of {{ill|Emperor Shōmu|ja|聖武天皇|12=draft}}, the shrine gained recognition in 732 CE (Tenpyō 4) for its efficacy in bringing rain during a severe drought. Similarly, during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Yōzei|ja|陽成天皇|12=draft}} in 884 CE (Gangyō 8), {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|Sugawara no Michizane|12=draft}} offered prayers during another drought, and the shrine was revered for its rain-bringing miracles. Since then, the shrine has also been called the "Rainfall deity Shrine." On August 14, 1906 (Meiji 39), under the {{ill|Shrine Consolidation Policy|ja|神社合祀|1=Shrine Consolidation Policy|12=draft}} directive, the {{ill|Furumoto Shrine|ja|古元神社|12=draft}} and {{ill|Ichinose Shrine|ja|一瀬神社|12=draft}}, both located in Habutaki, were merged with Ogami Shrine on November 11, 1906. == Cultural Assets == * Shrine plaque inscribed by {{ill|Kazanin Atsunaga|ja|花山院厚卿|12=draft}} == Auxiliary Shrines == * Itsukushima Shrine * Ogami Kojin Shrine == Shrine Surroundings == The shrine is located at the northern foothills of {{ill|Kōnoyama|ja|神於山|12=draft}}. The site includes the "Rainfall Waterfall" (雨降りの滝), which flows into a tributary of the Tsudagawa River. Due to its inland location, the region often experiences reduced rainfall after the rainy season, similar to the Seto Inland Sea climate. However, the shrine's surroundings are notably humid, supporting diverse vegetation, including rare warm-temperate broadleaf trees. The forest around the shrine consists of evergreen broadleaf species dominated by {{ill|Castanopsis cuspidata|ja|コジイ|12=draft}} and {{ill|Elaeocarpus japonicus|ja|ミミズバイ|12=draft}}, along with {{ill|Holtunok|ja|ホルトノキ|12=draft}}, {{ill|Ardisia japonica|ja|タイミンタチバナ|12=draft}}, and {{ill|Corchoropsis tomentosa|ja|ツルコウジ|12=draft}}. This area was designated a Natural Monument by Kishiwada City in 1968 (Shōwa 43). It is also listed among the {{ill|Osaka 100 Best Green Sites|ja|大阪みどりの百選|12=draft}} and was selected as an Osaka Prefecture Natural Conservation Area in 1989 (Heisei 1). However, the natural environment around the shrine, including Kōnoyama, has suffered significant degradation over the past 50 years. Restoration efforts are ongoing under the "Kōnoyama Area Natural Restoration Comprehensive Plan" (October 2004) and the "Kōnoyama Area Natural Restoration Project Implementation Plan" (June 2005), supervised by the Ministry of Agriculture, Forestry and Fisheries. == Access == - **By Bus**: From {{ill|Kishiwada Station|ja|岸和田駅|12=draft}} (Nankai Electric Railway) or {{ill|Higashi-Kishiwada Station|ja|東岸和田駅|12=draft}} (JR Hanwa Line), take the Nankai Wing Bus South on routes 0, 41, 42, or 43 to Miyano-dai. Buses run approximately twice per hour. - **By Car**: Use {{ill|Hanwa Expressway|ja|阪和自動車道|12=draft}}, exiting at either Kaizuka IC or Kishiwada-Izumi IC, and follow National Route 170. == References == {{Reflist}} {{Commons|Category:Ogami-jinja, Kishiwada}} {{Osaka 100 Best Green Sites}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shikinaisha in Izumi Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Kishiwada]] [[Category:Buildings in Kishiwada]] [[Category:Former village shrines in Japan]] [[Category:Okami Shrine (Kishiwada)]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:New]] }} [[sn:Okami Shrine (Kishiwada)]] [[ja:%E6%84%8F%E8%B3%80%E7%BE%8E%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE (%E5%B2%B8%E5%92%8C%E7%94%B0%E5%B8%82)]] [[ja:&action=edit]] [[ja:意賀美神社]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Ogami Shrine}}

It is also listed among the {{ill|Osaka 100 Best Green Sites|ja|大阪みどりの百選|12=draft|12=draft}} and was selected as an Osaka Prefecture Natural Conservation Area in 1989 (Heisei 1). However, the natural environment around the shrine, including Kōnoyama, has suffered significant degradation over the past 50 years. Restoration efforts are ongoing under the "Kōnoyama Area Natural Restoration Comprehensive Plan" (October 2004) and the "Kōnoyama Area Natural Restoration Project Implementation Plan" (June 2005), supervised by the Ministry of Agriculture, Forestry and Fisheries. == Access == - **By Bus**: From {{ill|Kishiwada Station|ja|岸和田駅|12=draft|12=draft}} (Nankai Electric Railway) or {{ill|Higashi-Kishiwada Station|ja|東岸和田駅|12=draft|12=draft}} (JR Hanwa Line), take the Nankai Wing Bus South on routes 0, 41, 42, or 43 to Miyano-dai. Buses run approximately twice per hour. - **By Car**: Use {{ill|Hanwa Expressway|ja|阪和自動車道|12=draft|12=draft}}, exiting at either Kaizuka IC or Kishiwada-Izumi IC, and follow National Route 170. == References == {{Reflist}} {{Commons|Category:Ogami-jinja, Kishiwada}} {{Osaka 100 Best Green Sites}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shikinaisha in Izumi Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Kishiwada]] [[Category:Buildings in Kishiwada]] [[Category:Son-sha]] [[Category:Okami Shrine (Kishiwada)]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:New]] }} [[sn:Okami Shrine (Kishiwada)]] [[ja:%E6%84%8F%E8%B3%80%E7%BE%8E%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE (%E5%B2%B8%E5%92%8C%E7%94%B0%E5%B8%82)]] [[ja:&action=edit]] [[ja:意賀美神社]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Ogami Shrine}}

revision.text:
{{Shrine | name = Ogami Shrine (意賀美神社) | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:OgamiJinjaHaiden.jpg|250px|意賀美神社拝殿]]
Haiden {{Maplink2|zoom=12|frame=yes|plain=yes|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|frame-height=220|type=point|frame-latitude=34.437|frame-longitude=135.40|coord={{coord|34|24|57.54|N|135|24|38.94|E}}}} | location = 17 Habutaki-cho, Kishiwada City, Osaka Prefecture, Japan | coordinates = {{Coord|34|24|57.54|N|135|24|38.94|E|region:JP-27_scale:30000|display=inline,title}} | Deities = Yamigami-no-Ōkami
Ichikishimahime-no-Mikoto
Hachidai Ryūō and Gozu Tennō
Itaki Iwa-no-Kami | Shrine_Rank = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=draft|12=draft}} (Minor), Former Village Shrine | Foundation = Before the {{ill|Tenpyō era|ja|天平|12=draft|12=draft}} (729–749) | Main_building = {{ill|Hafu-zukuri|ja|破風造|12=draft|12=draft}} | Festival = October 5 }} '''Ogami Shrine''' (意賀美神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社|12=draft|12=draft}} located in {{ill|Kishiwada|ja|岸和田市|12=draft|12=draft}}, Osaka Prefecture, Japan. It is a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=draft|12=draft}} (Minor Shrine) and was classified as a village shrine under the {{ill|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|ja|近代社格制度|1=Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|12=draft|12=draft}}. The shrine serves as the tutelary deity for the former Amakawataki and Habutaki villages and is located at an important water management site. == History == During the reign of {{ill|Emperor Shōmu|ja|聖武天皇|12=draft|12=draft}}, the shrine gained recognition in 732 CE (Tenpyō 4) for its efficacy in bringing rain during a severe drought. Similarly, during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Yōzei|ja|陽成天皇|12=draft|12=draft}} in 884 CE (Gangyō 8), {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|Sugawara no Michizane|12=draft|12=draft}} offered prayers during another drought, and the shrine was revered for its rain-bringing miracles. Since then, the shrine has also been called the "Rainfall deity Shrine." On August 14, 1906 (Meiji 39), under the {{ill|Shrine Consolidation Policy|ja|神社合祀|1=Shrine Consolidation Policy|12=draft|12=draft}} directive, the {{ill|Furumoto Shrine|ja|古元神社|12=draft|12=draft}} and {{ill|Ichinose Shrine|ja|一瀬神社|12=draft|12=draft}}, both located in Habutaki, were merged with Ogami Shrine on November 11, 1906. == Cultural Assets == * Shrine plaque inscribed by {{ill|Kazanin Atsunaga|ja|花山院厚卿|12=draft|12=draft}} == Auxiliary Shrines == * Itsukushima Shrine * Ogami Kojin Shrine == Shrine Surroundings == The shrine is located at the northern foothills of {{ill|Kōnoyama|ja|神於山|12=draft|12=draft}}. The site includes the "Rainfall Waterfall" (雨降りの滝), which flows into a tributary of the Tsudagawa River. Due to its inland location, the region often experiences reduced rainfall after the rainy season, similar to the Seto Inland Sea climate. However, the shrine's surroundings are notably humid, supporting diverse vegetation, including rare warm-temperate broadleaf trees. The forest around the shrine consists of evergreen broadleaf species dominated by {{ill|Castanopsis cuspidata|ja|コジイ|12=draft|12=draft}} and {{ill|Elaeocarpus japonicus|ja|ミミズバイ|12=draft|12=draft}}, along with {{ill|Holtunok|ja|ホルトノキ|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Ardisia japonica|ja|タイミンタチバナ|12=draft|12=draft}}, and {{ill|Corchoropsis tomentosa|ja|ツルコウジ|12=draft|12=draft}}. This area was designated a Natural Monument by Kishiwada City in 1968 (Shōwa 43). It is also listed among the {{ill|Osaka 100 Best Green Sites|ja|大阪みどりの百選|12=draft|12=draft}} and was selected as an Osaka Prefecture Natural Conservation Area in 1989 (Heisei 1). However, the natural environment around the shrine, including Kōnoyama, has suffered significant degradation over the past 50 years. Restoration efforts are ongoing under the "Kōnoyama Area Natural Restoration Comprehensive Plan" (October 2004) and the "Kōnoyama Area Natural Restoration Project Implementation Plan" (June 2005), supervised by the Ministry of Agriculture, Forestry and Fisheries. == Access == - **By Bus**: From {{ill|Kishiwada Station|ja|岸和田駅|12=draft|12=draft}} (Nankai Electric Railway) or {{ill|Higashi-Kishiwada Station|ja|東岸和田駅|12=draft|12=draft}} (JR Hanwa Line), take the Nankai Wing Bus South on routes 0, 41, 42, or 43 to Miyano-dai. Buses run approximately twice per hour. - **By Car**: Use {{ill|Hanwa Expressway|ja|阪和自動車道|12=draft|12=draft}}, exiting at either Kaizuka IC or Kishiwada-Izumi IC, and follow National Route 170. == References == {{Reflist}} {{Commons|Category:Ogami-jinja, Kishiwada}} {{Osaka 100 Best Green Sites}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shikinaisha in Izumi Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Kishiwada]] [[Category:Buildings in Kishiwada]] [[Category:Son-sha]] [[Category:Okami Shrine (Kishiwada)]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:New]] }} [[sn:Okami Shrine (Kishiwada)]] [[ja:%E6%84%8F%E8%B3%80%E7%BE%8E%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE (%E5%B2%B8%E5%92%8C%E7%94%B0%E5%B8%82)]] [[ja:&action=edit]] [[ja:意賀美神社]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Ogami Shrine}}

Buses run approximately twice per hour. - **By Car**: Use {{ill|Hanwa Expressway|ja|阪和自動車道|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, exiting at either Kaizuka IC or Kishiwada-Izumi IC, and follow National Route 170. == References == {{Reflist}} {{Commons|Category:Ogami-jinja, Kishiwada}} {{Osaka 100 Best Green Sites}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{translated page|ja|意賀美神社 (岸和田市)|version=97795615|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Ogami Shrine}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shikinaisha in Izumi Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Kishiwada]] [[Category:Buildings in Kishiwada]] [[Category:Son-sha]] [[Category:Okami Shrine (Kishiwada)]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] }} [[sn:Okami Shrine (Kishiwada)]] [[ja:%E6%84%8F%E8%B3%80%E7%BE%8E%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE (%E5%B2%B8%E5%92%8C%E7%94%B0%E5%B8%82)]] [[ja:&action=edit]] [[ja:意賀美神社]]

revision.text:
{{Shrine | name = Ogami Shrine (意賀美神社) | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:OgamiJinjaHaiden.jpg|250px|意賀美神社拝殿]]
Haiden {{Maplink2|zoom=12|frame=yes|plain=yes|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|frame-height=220|type=point|frame-latitude=34.437|frame-longitude=135.40|coord={{coord|34|24|57.54|N|135|24|38.94|E}}}} | location = 17 Habutaki-cho, Kishiwada City, Osaka Prefecture, Japan | coordinates = {{Coord|34|24|57.54|N|135|24|38.94|E|region:JP-27_scale:30000|display=inline,title}} | Deities = Yamigami-no-Ōkami
Ichikishima-hime-no-Mikoto
Hachidai Ryūō and Gozu Tennō
Itaki Iwa-no-Kami | Shrine_Rank = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Minor), Former Village Shrine | Foundation = Before the {{ill|Tenpyō era|ja|天平|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (729–749) | Main_building = {{ill|Hafu-zukuri|ja|破風造|qq=draft|qq=draft}} | Festival = October 5 }} '''Ogami Shrine''' (意賀美神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} located in {{ill|Kishiwada|ja|岸和田市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, Osaka Prefecture, Japan. It is a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Minor Shrine) and was classified as a village shrine under the {{ill|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|ja|近代社格制度|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|1=Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. The shrine serves as the tutelary deity for the former Amakawataki and Habutaki villages and is located at an important water management site. == History == During the reign of {{ill|Emperor Shōmu|ja|聖武天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, the shrine gained recognition in 732 CE (Tenpyō 4) for its efficacy in bringing rain during a severe drought. Similarly, during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Yōzei|ja|陽成天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft}} in 884 CE (Gangyō 8), {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|Sugawara no Michizane|qq=draft|qq=draft}} offered prayers during another drought, and the shrine was revered for its rain-bringing miracles. Since then, the shrine has also been called the "Rainfall deity Shrine." On August 14, 1906 (Meiji 39), under the {{ill|Shrine Consolidation Policy|ja|神社合祀|1=Shrine Consolidation Policy|qq=draft|qq=draft}} directive, the {{ill|Furumoto Shrine|ja|古元神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and {{ill|Ichinose Shrine|ja|一瀬神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, both located in Habutaki, were merged with Ogami Shrine on November 11, 1906. == Cultural Assets == * Shrine plaque inscribed by {{ill|Kazanin Atsunaga|ja|花山院厚卿|qq=draft|qq=draft}} == Auxiliary Shrines == * Itsukushima Shrine * Ogami Kojin Shrine == Shrine Surroundings == The shrine is located at the northern foothills of {{ill|Kōnoyama|ja|神於山|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. The site includes the "Rainfall Waterfall" (雨降りの滝), which flows into a tributary of the Tsudagawa River. Due to its inland location, the region often experiences reduced rainfall after the rainy season, similar to the Seto Inland Sea climate. However, the shrine's surroundings are notably humid, supporting diverse vegetation, including rare warm-temperate broadleaf trees. The forest around the shrine consists of evergreen broadleaf species dominated by {{ill|Castanopsis cuspidata|ja|コジイ|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and {{ill|Elaeocarpus japonicus|ja|ミミズバイ|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, along with {{ill|Holtunok|ja|ホルトノキ|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Ardisia japonica|ja|タイミンタチバナ|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, and {{ill|Corchoropsis tomentosa|ja|ツルコウジ|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. This area was designated a Natural Monument by Kishiwada City in 1968 (Shōwa 43). It is also listed among the {{ill|Osaka 100 Best Green Sites|ja|大阪みどりの百選|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and was selected as an Osaka Prefecture Natural Conservation Area in 1989 (Heisei 1). However, the natural environment around the shrine, including Kōnoyama, has suffered significant degradation over the past 50 years. Restoration efforts are ongoing under the "Kōnoyama Area Natural Restoration Comprehensive Plan" (October 2004) and the "Kōnoyama Area Natural Restoration Project Implementation Plan" (June 2005), supervised by the Ministry of Agriculture, Forestry and Fisheries. == Access == - **By Bus**: From {{ill|Kishiwada Station|ja|岸和田駅|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Nankai Electric Railway) or {{ill|Higashi-Kishiwada Station|ja|東岸和田駅|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (JR Hanwa Line), take the Nankai Wing Bus South on routes 0, 41, 42, or 43 to Miyano-dai. Buses run approximately twice per hour. - **By Car**: Use {{ill|Hanwa Expressway|ja|阪和自動車道|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, exiting at either Kaizuka IC or Kishiwada-Izumi IC, and follow National Route 170. == References == {{Reflist}} {{Commons|Category:Ogami-jinja, Kishiwada}} {{Osaka 100 Best Green Sites}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{translated page|ja|意賀美神社 (岸和田市)|version=97795615|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Ogami Shrine}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shikinaisha in Izumi Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Kishiwada]] [[Category:Buildings in Kishiwada]] [[Category:Son-sha]] [[Category:Okami Shrine (Kishiwada)]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] }} [[sn:Okami Shrine (Kishiwada)]] [[ja:%E6%84%8F%E8%B3%80%E7%BE%8E%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE (%E5%B2%B8%E5%92%8C%E7%94%B0%E5%B8%82)]] [[ja:&action=edit]] [[ja:意賀美神社]]
