The forest around the shrine consists of evergreen broadleaf species dominated by {{ill|Castanopsis cuspidata|ja|コジイ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Castanopsis cuspidata|qq=}} and {{ill|Elaeocarpus japonicus|ja|ミミズバイ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Elaeocarpus japonicus|qq=}}, along with {{ill|Holtunok|ja|ホルトノキ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Holtunok|qq=}}, {{ill|Ardisia japonica|ja|タイミンタチバナ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ardisia japonica|qq=}}, and {{ill|Corchoropsis tomentosa|ja|ツルコウジ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Corchoropsis tomentosa|qq=}}. This area was designated a Natural Monument by Kishiwada City in 1968 (Shōwa 43). It is also listed among the {{ill|Osaka 100 Best Green Sites|ja|大阪みどりの百選|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Osaka 100 Best Green Sites|qq=}} and was selected as an Osaka Prefecture Natural Conservation Area in 1989 (Heisei 1). However, the natural environment around the shrine, including Kōnoyama, has suffered significant degradation over the past 50 years. Restoration efforts are ongoing under the "Kōnoyama Area Natural Restoration Comprehensive Plan" (October 2004) and the "Kōnoyama Area Natural Restoration Project Implementation Plan" (June 2005), supervised by the Ministry of Agriculture, Forestry and Fisheries. == Access == - **By Bus**: From {{ill|Kishiwada Station|ja|岸和田駅|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kishiwada Station|qq=}} (Nankai Electric Railway) or {{ill|Higashi-Kishiwada Station|ja|東岸和田駅|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Higashi-Kishiwada Station|qq=}} (JR Hanwa Line), take the Nankai Wing Bus South on routes 0, 41, 42, or 43 to Miyano-dai. Buses run approximately twice per hour. - **By Car**: Use {{ill|Hanwa Expressway|ja|阪和自動車道|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hanwa Expressway|qq=}}, exiting at either Kaizuka IC or Kishiwada-Izumi IC, and follow National Route 170. == References == {{Reflist}} {{Commons|Category:Ogami-jinja, Kishiwada}} {{Osaka 100 Best Green Sites}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{translated page|ja|意賀美神社 (岸和田市)|version=97795615|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shikinaisha in Izumi Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Kishiwada]] [[Category:Buildings in Kishiwada]] [[Category:Son-sha]] [[Category:Okami Shrine (Kishiwada)]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] }} [[sn:Okami Shrine (Kishiwada)]] [[ja:%E6%84%8F%E8%B3%80%E7%BE%8E%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE (%E5%B2%B8%E5%92%8C%E7%94%B0%E5%B8%82)]] [[ja:&action=edit]] [[ja:意賀美神社]]

Ichikishima-hime-no-Mikoto
Hachidai Ryūō and Gozu Tennō
Itaki Iwa-no-Kami | Shrine_Rank = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|qq=}} (Minor), Former Village Shrine | Foundation = Before the {{ill|Tenpyō era|ja|天平|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenpyō era|qq=}} (729–749) | Main_building = {{ill|Hafu-zukuri|ja|破風造|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hafu-zukuri|qq=}} | Festival = October 5 }} '''Ogami Shrine''' (意賀美神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinto shrine|qq=}} located in {{ill|Kishiwada|ja|岸和田市|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kishiwada|qq=}}, Osaka Prefecture, Japan. It is a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|qq=}} (Minor Shrine) and was classified as a village shrine under the {{ill|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|ja|近代社格制度|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|1=Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|13=User:Immanuelle/Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|qq=}}. The shrine serves as the tutelary deity for the former Amakawataki and Habutaki villages and is located at an important water management site. == History == During the reign of {{ill|Emperor Shōmu|ja|聖武天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Shōmu|qq=}}, the shrine gained recognition in 732 CE (Tenpyō 4) for its efficacy in bringing rain during a severe drought. Similarly, during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Yōzei|ja|陽成天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Yōzei|qq=}} in 884 CE (Gangyō 8), {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|Sugawara no Michizane|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara no Michizane|qq=}} offered prayers during another drought, and the shrine was revered for its rain-bringing miracles. Since then, the shrine has also been called the "Rainfall deity Shrine." On August 14, 1906 (Meiji 39), under the {{ill|Shrine Consolidation Policy|ja|神社合祀|1=Shrine Consolidation Policy|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shrine Consolidation Policy|13=User:Immanuelle/Shrine Consolidation Policy|qq=}} directive, the {{ill|Furumoto Shrine|ja|古元神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Furumoto Shrine|qq=}} and {{ill|Ichinose Shrine|ja|一瀬神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichinose Shrine|qq=}}, both located in Habutaki, were merged with Ogami Shrine on November 11, 1906. == Cultural Assets == * Shrine plaque inscribed by {{ill|Kazanin Atsunaga|ja|花山院厚卿|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kazanin Atsunaga|qq=}} == Auxiliary Shrines == * Itsukushima Shrine * Ogami Kojin Shrine == Shrine Surroundings == The shrine is located at the northern foothills of {{ill|Kōnoyama|ja|神於山|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōnoyama|qq=}}. The site includes the "Rainfall Waterfall" (雨降りの滝), which flows into a tributary of the Tsudagawa River. Due to its inland location, the region often experiences reduced rainfall after the rainy season, similar to the Seto Inland Sea climate. However, the shrine's surroundings are notably humid, supporting diverse vegetation, including rare warm-temperate broadleaf trees. The forest around the shrine consists of evergreen broadleaf species dominated by {{ill|Castanopsis cuspidata|ja|コジイ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Castanopsis cuspidata|qq=}} and {{ill|Elaeocarpus japonicus|ja|ミミズバイ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Elaeocarpus japonicus|qq=}}, along with {{ill|Holtunok|ja|ホルトノキ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Holtunok|qq=}}, {{ill|Ardisia japonica|ja|タイミンタチバナ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ardisia japonica|qq=}}, and {{ill|Corchoropsis tomentosa|ja|ツルコウジ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Corchoropsis tomentosa|qq=}}. This area was designated a Natural Monument by Kishiwada City in 1968 (Shōwa 43). It is also listed among the {{ill|Osaka 100 Best Green Sites|ja|大阪みどりの百選|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Osaka 100 Best Green Sites|qq=}} and was selected as an Osaka Prefecture Natural Conservation Area in 1989 (Heisei 1). However, the natural environment around the shrine, including Kōnoyama, has suffered significant degradation over the past 50 years. Restoration efforts are ongoing under the "Kōnoyama Area Natural Restoration Comprehensive Plan" (October 2004) and the "Kōnoyama Area Natural Restoration Project Implementation Plan" (June 2005), supervised by the Ministry of Agriculture, Forestry and Fisheries. == Access == - **By Bus**: From {{ill|Kishiwada Station|ja|岸和田駅|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kishiwada Station|qq=}} (Nankai Electric Railway) or {{ill|Higashi-Kishiwada Station|ja|東岸和田駅|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Higashi-Kishiwada Station|qq=}} (JR Hanwa Line), take the Nankai Wing Bus South on routes 0, 41, 42, or 43 to Miyano-dai. Buses run approximately twice per hour. - **By Car**: Use {{ill|Hanwa Expressway|ja|阪和自動車道|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hanwa Expressway|qq=}}, exiting at either Kaizuka IC or Kishiwada-Izumi IC, and follow National Route 170. == References == {{Reflist}} {{Commons|Category:Ogami-jinja, Kishiwada}} {{Osaka 100 Best Green Sites}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{translated page|ja|意賀美神社 (岸和田市)|version=97795615|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shikinaisha in Izumi Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Kishiwada]] [[Category:Buildings in Kishiwada]] [[Category:Son-sha]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] }} [[sn:Okami Shrine (Kishiwada)]] [[ja:%E6%84%8F%E8%B3%80%E7%BE%8E%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE (%E5%B2%B8%E5%92%8C%E7%94%B0%E5%B8%82)]] [[ja:&action=edit]] [[ja:意賀美神社]]

Itaki Iwa-no-Kami | Shrine_Rank = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|qq=}} (Minor), Former Village Shrine | Foundation = Before the [[:en:Tenpyō|Tenpyō era]] (729–749) | Main_building = {{ill|Hafu-zukuri|ja|破風造|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hafu-zukuri|qq=}} | Festival = October 5 }} '''Ogami Shrine''' (意賀美神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Kishiwada, Osaka|Kishiwada]], Osaka Prefecture, Japan. It is a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|qq=}} (Minor Shrine) and was classified as a village shrine under the [[:en:Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines]]. The shrine serves as the tutelary deity for the former Amakawataki and Habutaki villages and is located at an important water management site. == History == During the reign of [[:en:Emperor Shōmu|Emperor Shōmu]], the shrine gained recognition in 732 CE (Tenpyō 4) for its efficacy in bringing rain during a severe drought. Similarly, during the reign of [[:en:Emperor Yōzei|Emperor Yōzei]] in 884 CE (Gangyō 8), {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|Sugawara no Michizane|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara no Michizane|qq=}} offered prayers during another drought, and the shrine was revered for its rain-bringing miracles. Since then, the shrine has also been called the "Rainfall deity Shrine." On August 14, 1906 (Meiji 39), under the [[:en:Shrine Consolidation Policy|Shrine Consolidation Policy]] directive, the {{ill|Furumoto Shrine|ja|古元神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Furumoto Shrine|qq=}} and {{ill|Ichinose Shrine|ja|一瀬神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichinose Shrine|qq=}}, both located in Habutaki, were merged with Ogami Shrine on November 11, 1906. == Cultural Assets == * Shrine plaque inscribed by {{ill|Kazanin Atsunaga|ja|花山院厚卿|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kazanin Atsunaga|qq=}} == Auxiliary Shrines == * Itsukushima Shrine * Ogami Kojin Shrine == Shrine Surroundings == The shrine is located at the northern foothills of {{ill|Kōnoyama|ja|神於山|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōnoyama|qq=}}. The site includes the "Rainfall Waterfall" (雨降りの滝), which flows into a tributary of the Tsudagawa River. Due to its inland location, the region often experiences reduced rainfall after the rainy season, similar to the Seto Inland Sea climate. However, the shrine's surroundings are notably humid, supporting diverse vegetation, including rare warm-temperate broadleaf trees. The forest around the shrine consists of evergreen broadleaf species dominated by {{ill|Castanopsis cuspidata|ja|コジイ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Castanopsis cuspidata|qq=}} and {{ill|Elaeocarpus japonicus|ja|ミミズバイ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Elaeocarpus japonicus|qq=}}, along with [[:en:Elaeocarpus sylvestris|Holtunok]], {{ill|Ardisia japonica|ja|タイミンタチバナ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ardisia japonica|qq=}}, and {{ill|Corchoropsis tomentosa|ja|ツルコウジ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Corchoropsis tomentosa|qq=}}. This area was designated a Natural Monument by Kishiwada City in 1968 (Shōwa 43). It is also listed among the {{ill|Osaka 100 Best Green Sites|ja|大阪みどりの百選|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Osaka 100 Best Green Sites|qq=}} and was selected as an Osaka Prefecture Natural Conservation Area in 1989 (Heisei 1). However, the natural environment around the shrine, including Kōnoyama, has suffered significant degradation over the past 50 years. Restoration efforts are ongoing under the "Kōnoyama Area Natural Restoration Comprehensive Plan" (October 2004) and the "Kōnoyama Area Natural Restoration Project Implementation Plan" (June 2005), supervised by the Ministry of Agriculture, Forestry and Fisheries. == Access == - **By Bus**: From [[:en:Kishiwada Station|Kishiwada Station]] (Nankai Electric Railway) or [[:en:Higashi-Kishiwada Station|Higashi-Kishiwada Station]] (JR Hanwa Line), take the Nankai Wing Bus South on routes 0, 41, 42, or 43 to Miyano-dai. Buses run approximately twice per hour. - **By Car**: Use [[:en:Hanwa Expressway|Hanwa Expressway]], exiting at either Kaizuka IC or Kishiwada-Izumi IC, and follow National Route 170. == References == {{Reflist}} {{Commons|Category:Ogami-jinja, Kishiwada}} {{Osaka 100 Best Green Sites}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{translated page|ja|意賀美神社 (岸和田市)|version=97795615|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shikinaisha in Izumi Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Kishiwada]] [[Category:Buildings in Kishiwada]] [[Category:Son-sha]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] }} [[sn:Okami Shrine (Kishiwada)]] [[ja:%E6%84%8F%E8%B3%80%E7%BE%8E%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE (%E5%B2%B8%E5%92%8C%E7%94%B0%E5%B8%82)]] [[ja:&action=edit]] [[ja:意賀美神社]] [[Category:大阪府の旧村社]] [[Category:岸和田市の建築物]]

Buses run approximately twice per hour. - **By Car**: Use [[:en:Hanwa Expressway|Hanwa Expressway]], exiting at either Kaizuka IC or Kishiwada-Izumi IC, and follow National Route 170. == References == {{Reflist}} {{Commons|Category:Ogami-jinja, Kishiwada}} {{Osaka 100 Best Green Sites}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{translated page|ja|意賀美神社 (岸和田市)|version=97795615|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shikinaisha in Izumi Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Kishiwada]] [[Category:Buildings in Kishiwada]] [[Category:Son-sha]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] }} [[sn:Okami Shrine (Kishiwada)]] [[ja:%E6%84%8F%E8%B3%80%E7%BE%8E%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE (%E5%B2%B8%E5%92%8C%E7%94%B0%E5%B8%82)]] [[ja:&action=edit]] [[ja:意賀美神社]] [[Category:大阪府の旧村社]] [[Category:Buildings in Kishiwada, Osaka]] [[Category:History of Kishiwada, Osaka]]

{{Shrine | name = Ogami Shrine (意賀美神社) | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:OgamiJinjaHaiden.jpg|250px|意賀美神社拝殿]]
Haiden {{Maplink2|zoom=12|frame=yes|plain=yes|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|frame-height=220|type=point|frame-latitude=34.437|frame-longitude=135.40|coord={{coord|34|24|57.54|N|135|24|38.94|E}}}} | location = 17 Habutaki-cho, Kishiwada City, Osaka Prefecture, Japan | coordinates = {{Coord|34|24|57.54|N|135|24|38.94|E|region:JP-27_scale:30000|display=inline,title}} | Deities = Yamigami-no-Ōkami
Ichikishima-hime-no-Mikoto
Hachidai Ryūō and Gozu Tennō
Itaki Iwa-no-Kami | Shrine_Rank = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|qq=}} (Minor), Former Village Shrine | Foundation = Before the [[:en:Tenpyō|Tenpyō era]] (729–749) | Main_building = {{ill|Hafu-zukuri|ja|破風造|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hafu-zukuri|qq=}} | Festival = October 5 }} '''Ogami Shrine''' (意賀美神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Kishiwada, Osaka|Kishiwada]], Osaka Prefecture, Japan. It is a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|qq=}} (Minor Shrine) and was classified as a village shrine under the [[:en:Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines]]. The shrine serves as the tutelary deity for the former Amakawataki and Habutaki villages and is located at an important water management site. == History == During the reign of [[:en:Emperor Shōmu|Emperor Shōmu]], the shrine gained recognition in 732 CE (Tenpyō 4) for its efficacy in bringing rain during a severe drought. Similarly, during the reign of [[:en:Emperor Yōzei|Emperor Yōzei]] in 884 CE (Gangyō 8), {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|Sugawara no Michizane|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara no Michizane|qq=}} offered prayers during another drought, and the shrine was revered for its rain-bringing miracles. Since then, the shrine has also been called the "Rainfall deity Shrine." On August 14, 1906 (Meiji 39), under the [[:en:Shrine Consolidation Policy|Shrine Consolidation Policy]] directive, the {{ill|Furumoto Shrine|ja|古元神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Furumoto Shrine|qq=}} and {{ill|Ichinose Shrine|ja|一瀬神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichinose Shrine|qq=}}, both located in Habutaki, were merged with Ogami Shrine on November 11, 1906. == Cultural Assets == * Shrine plaque inscribed by {{ill|Kazanin Atsunaga|ja|花山院厚卿|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kazanin Atsunaga|qq=}} == Auxiliary Shrines == * Itsukushima Shrine * Ogami Kojin Shrine == Shrine Surroundings == The shrine is located at the northern foothills of {{ill|Kōnoyama|ja|神於山|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōnoyama|qq=}}. The site includes the "Rainfall Waterfall" (雨降りの滝), which flows into a tributary of the Tsudagawa River. Due to its inland location, the region often experiences reduced rainfall after the rainy season, similar to the Seto Inland Sea climate. However, the shrine's surroundings are notably humid, supporting diverse vegetation, including rare warm-temperate broadleaf trees. The forest around the shrine consists of evergreen broadleaf species dominated by {{ill|Castanopsis cuspidata|ja|コジイ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Castanopsis cuspidata|qq=}} and {{ill|Elaeocarpus japonicus|ja|ミミズバイ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Elaeocarpus japonicus|qq=}}, along with [[:en:Elaeocarpus sylvestris|Holtunok]], {{ill|Ardisia japonica|ja|タイミンタチバナ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ardisia japonica|qq=}}, and {{ill|Corchoropsis tomentosa|ja|ツルコウジ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Corchoropsis tomentosa|qq=}}. This area was designated a Natural Monument by Kishiwada City in 1968 (Shōwa 43). It is also listed among the {{ill|Osaka 100 Best Green Sites|ja|大阪みどりの百選|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Osaka 100 Best Green Sites|qq=}} and was selected as an Osaka Prefecture Natural Conservation Area in 1989 (Heisei 1). However, the natural environment around the shrine, including Kōnoyama, has suffered significant degradation over the past 50 years. Restoration efforts are ongoing under the "Kōnoyama Area Natural Restoration Comprehensive Plan" (October 2004) and the "Kōnoyama Area Natural Restoration Project Implementation Plan" (June 2005), supervised by the Ministry of Agriculture, Forestry and Fisheries. == Access == - **By Bus**: From [[:en:Kishiwada Station|Kishiwada Station]] (Nankai Electric Railway) or [[:en:Higashi-Kishiwada Station|Higashi-Kishiwada Station]] (JR Hanwa Line), take the Nankai Wing Bus South on routes 0, 41, 42, or 43 to Miyano-dai. Buses run approximately twice per hour. - **By Car**: Use [[:en:Hanwa Expressway|Hanwa Expressway]], exiting at either Kaizuka IC or Kishiwada-Izumi IC, and follow National Route 170. == References == {{Reflist}} {{Commons|Category:Ogami-jinja, Kishiwada}} {{Osaka 100 Best Green Sites}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{translated page|ja|意賀美神社 (岸和田市)|version=97795615|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shikinaisha in Izumi Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Kishiwada]] [[Category:Buildings in Kishiwada]] [[Category:Son-sha]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] }} [[sn:Okami Shrine (Kishiwada)]] [[ja:%E6%84%8F%E8%B3%80%E7%BE%8E%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE (%E5%B2%B8%E5%92%8C%E7%94%B0%E5%B8%82)]] [[ja:&action=edit]] [[ja:意賀美神社]] [[Category:大阪府の旧村社]] [[Category:Buildings in Kishiwada, Osaka]] [[Category:History of Kishiwada, Osaka]] {{Commons category|Ogami-jinja, Kishiwada}} [[ja:意賀美神社 (岸和田市)]]

Similarly, during the reign of [[:en:Emperor Yōzei|Emperor Yōzei]] in 884 CE (Gangyō 8), {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|Sugawara no Michizane|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara no Michizane|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} offered prayers during another drought, and the shrine was revered for its rain-bringing miracles. Since then, the shrine has also been called the "Rainfall deity Shrine." On August 14, 1906 (Meiji 39), under the [[:en:Shrine Consolidation Policy|Shrine Consolidation Policy]] directive, the {{ill|Furumoto Shrine|ja|古元神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Furumoto Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and {{ill|Ichinose Shrine|ja|一瀬神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichinose Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, both located in Habutaki, were merged with Ogami Shrine on November 11, 1906. == Cultural Assets == * Shrine plaque inscribed by {{ill|Kazanin Atsunaga|ja|花山院厚卿|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kazanin Atsunaga|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} == Auxiliary Shrines == * Itsukushima Shrine * Ogami Kojin Shrine == Shrine Surroundings == The shrine is located at the northern foothills of {{ill|Kōnoyama|ja|神於山|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōnoyama|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. The site includes the "Rainfall Waterfall" (雨降りの滝), which flows into a tributary of the Tsudagawa River. Due to its inland location, the region often experiences reduced rainfall after the rainy season, similar to the Seto Inland Sea climate. However, the shrine's surroundings are notably humid, supporting diverse vegetation, including rare warm-temperate broadleaf trees. The forest around the shrine consists of evergreen broadleaf species dominated by {{ill|Castanopsis cuspidata|ja|コジイ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Castanopsis cuspidata|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ツブラジイ}} and {{ill|Elaeocarpus japonicus|ja|ミミズバイ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Elaeocarpus japonicus|qq=}}, along with [[:en:Elaeocarpus sylvestris|Holtunok]], {{ill|Ardisia japonica|ja|タイミンタチバナ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ardisia japonica|qq=}}, and {{ill|Corchoropsis tomentosa|ja|ツルコウジ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Corchoropsis tomentosa|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. This area was designated a Natural Monument by Kishiwada City in 1968 (Shōwa 43). It is also listed among the {{ill|Osaka 100 Best Green Sites|ja|大阪みどりの百選|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Osaka 100 Best Green Sites|qq=}} and was selected as an Osaka Prefecture Natural Conservation Area in 1989 (Heisei 1). However, the natural environment around the shrine, including Kōnoyama, has suffered significant degradation over the past 50 years. Restoration efforts are ongoing under the "Kōnoyama Area Natural Restoration Comprehensive Plan" (October 2004) and the "Kōnoyama Area Natural Restoration Project Implementation Plan" (June 2005), supervised by the Ministry of Agriculture, Forestry and Fisheries. == Access == - **By Bus**: From [[:en:Kishiwada Station|Kishiwada Station]] (Nankai Electric Railway) or [[:en:Higashi-Kishiwada Station|Higashi-Kishiwada Station]] (JR Hanwa Line), take the Nankai Wing Bus South on routes 0, 41, 42, or 43 to Miyano-dai. Buses run approximately twice per hour. - **By Car**: Use [[:en:Hanwa Expressway|Hanwa Expressway]], exiting at either Kaizuka IC or Kishiwada-Izumi IC, and follow National Route 170. == References == {{Reflist}} {{Commons|Category:Ogami-jinja, Kishiwada}} {{Osaka 100 Best Green Sites}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{translated page|ja|意賀美神社 (岸和田市)|version=97795615|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shikinaisha in Izumi Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:Son-sha]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] }} [[sn:Okami Shrine (Kishiwada)]] [[ja:%E6%84%8F%E8%B3%80%E7%BE%8E%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE (%E5%B2%B8%E5%92%8C%E7%94%B0%E5%B8%82)]] [[ja:&action=edit]] [[ja:意賀美神社]] [[Category:大阪府の旧村社]] {{Commons category|Ogami-jinja, Kishiwada}} [[ja:意賀美神社 (岸和田市)]]

The forest around the shrine consists of evergreen broadleaf species dominated by {{ill|Castanopsis cuspidata|ja|コジイ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Castanopsis cuspidata|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ツブラジイ}} and {{ill|Elaeocarpus japonicus|ja|ミミズバイ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Elaeocarpus japonicus|qq=}}, along with [[:en:Elaeocarpus sylvestris|Holtunok]], {{ill|Ardisia japonica|ja|タイミンタチバナ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ardisia japonica|qq=}}, and {{ill|Corchoropsis tomentosa|ja|ツルコウジ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Corchoropsis tomentosa|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. This area was designated a Natural Monument by Kishiwada City in 1968 (Shōwa 43). It is also listed among the {{ill|Osaka 100 Best Green Sites|ja|大阪みどりの百選|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Osaka 100 Best Green Sites|qq=}} and was selected as an Osaka Prefecture Natural Conservation Area in 1989 (Heisei 1). However, the natural environment around the shrine, including Kōnoyama, has suffered significant degradation over the past 50 years. Restoration efforts are ongoing under the "Kōnoyama Area Natural Restoration Comprehensive Plan" (October 2004) and the "Kōnoyama Area Natural Restoration Project Implementation Plan" (June 2005), supervised by the Ministry of Agriculture, Forestry and Fisheries. == Access == - **By Bus**: From [[:en:Kishiwada Station|Kishiwada Station]] (Nankai Electric Railway) or [[:en:Higashi-Kishiwada Station|Higashi-Kishiwada Station]] (JR Hanwa Line), take the Nankai Wing Bus South on routes 0, 41, 42, or 43 to Miyano-dai. Buses run approximately twice per hour. - **By Car**: Use [[:en:Hanwa Expressway|Hanwa Expressway]], exiting at either Kaizuka IC or Kishiwada-Izumi IC, and follow National Route 170. == References == {{Reflist}} {{Commons|Category:Ogami-jinja, Kishiwada}} {{Osaka 100 Best Green Sites}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{translated page|ja|意賀美神社 (岸和田市)|version=97795615|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shikinaisha in Izumi Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:Son-sha]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] }} [[sn:Okami Shrine (Kishiwada)]] [[ja:%E6%84%8F%E8%B3%80%E7%BE%8E%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE (%E5%B2%B8%E5%92%8C%E7%94%B0%E5%B8%82)]] [[ja:&action=edit]] [[ja:意賀美神社]] [[Category:Son-sha in Osaka Prefecture]] {{Commons category|Ogami-jinja, Kishiwada}} [[ja:意賀美神社 (岸和田市)]] [[Category:paes with no wikidata]]

{{afc comment|Wikidata entry (none) Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:44, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Shrine | name = Ogami Shrine (意賀美神社) | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:OgamiJinjaHaiden.jpg|250px|意賀美神社拝殿]]
Haiden {{Maplink2|zoom=12|frame=yes|plain=yes|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|frame-height=220|type=point|frame-latitude=34.437|frame-longitude=135.40|coord={{coord|34|24|57.54|N|135|24|38.94|E}}}} | location = 17 Habutaki-cho, Kishiwada City, Osaka Prefecture, Japan | coordinates = {{Coord|34|24|57.54|N|135|24|38.94|E|region:JP-27_scale:30000|display=inline,title}} | Deities = Yamigami-no-Ōkami
Ichikishima-hime-no-Mikoto
Hachidai Ryūō and Gozu Tennō
Itaki Iwa-no-Kami | Shrine_Rank = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} (Minor), Former Village Shrine | Foundation = Before the [[:en:Tenpyō|Tenpyō era]] (729–749) | Main_building = {{ill|Hafu-zukuri|ja|破風造|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hafu-zukuri|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} | Festival = October 5 }} '''Ogami Shrine''' (意賀美神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Kishiwada, Osaka|Kishiwada]], Osaka Prefecture, Japan. It is a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} (Minor Shrine) and was classified as a village shrine under the [[:en:Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines]]. The shrine serves as the tutelary deity for the former Amakawataki and Habutaki villages and is located at an important water management site. == History == During the reign of [[:en:Emperor Shōmu|Emperor Shōmu]], the shrine gained recognition in 732 CE (Tenpyō 4) for its efficacy in bringing rain during a severe drought. Similarly, during the reign of [[:en:Emperor Yōzei|Emperor Yōzei]] in 884 CE (Gangyō 8), {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|Sugawara no Michizane|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara no Michizane|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} offered prayers during another drought, and the shrine was revered for its rain-bringing miracles. Since then, the shrine has also been called the "Rainfall deity Shrine." On August 14, 1906 (Meiji 39), under the [[:en:Shrine Consolidation Policy|Shrine Consolidation Policy]] directive, the {{ill|Furumoto Shrine|ja|古元神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Furumoto Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and {{ill|Ichinose Shrine|ja|一瀬神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichinose Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, both located in Habutaki, were merged with Ogami Shrine on November 11, 1906. == Cultural Assets == * Shrine plaque inscribed by {{ill|Kazanin Atsunaga|ja|花山院厚卿|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kazanin Atsunaga|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} == Auxiliary Shrines == * Itsukushima Shrine * Ogami Kojin Shrine == Shrine Surroundings == The shrine is located at the northern foothills of {{ill|Kōnoyama|ja|神於山|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōnoyama|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. The site includes the "Rainfall Waterfall" (雨降りの滝), which flows into a tributary of the Tsudagawa River. Due to its inland location, the region often experiences reduced rainfall after the rainy season, similar to the Seto Inland Sea climate. However, the shrine's surroundings are notably humid, supporting diverse vegetation, including rare warm-temperate broadleaf trees. The forest around the shrine consists of evergreen broadleaf species dominated by {{ill|Castanopsis cuspidata|ja|コジイ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Castanopsis cuspidata|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ツブラジイ}} and {{ill|Elaeocarpus japonicus|ja|ミミズバイ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Elaeocarpus japonicus|qq=}}, along with [[:en:Elaeocarpus sylvestris|Holtunok]], {{ill|Ardisia japonica|ja|タイミンタチバナ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ardisia japonica|qq=}}, and {{ill|Corchoropsis tomentosa|ja|ツルコウジ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Corchoropsis tomentosa|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. This area was designated a Natural Monument by Kishiwada City in 1968 (Shōwa 43). It is also listed among the {{ill|Osaka 100 Best Green Sites|ja|大阪みどりの百選|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Osaka 100 Best Green Sites|qq=}} and was selected as an Osaka Prefecture Natural Conservation Area in 1989 (Heisei 1). However, the natural environment around the shrine, including Kōnoyama, has suffered significant degradation over the past 50 years. Restoration efforts are ongoing under the "Kōnoyama Area Natural Restoration Comprehensive Plan" (October 2004) and the "Kōnoyama Area Natural Restoration Project Implementation Plan" (June 2005), supervised by the Ministry of Agriculture, Forestry and Fisheries. == Access == - **By Bus**: From [[:en:Kishiwada Station|Kishiwada Station]] (Nankai Electric Railway) or [[:en:Higashi-Kishiwada Station|Higashi-Kishiwada Station]] (JR Hanwa Line), take the Nankai Wing Bus South on routes 0, 41, 42, or 43 to Miyano-dai. Buses run approximately twice per hour. - **By Car**: Use [[:en:Hanwa Expressway|Hanwa Expressway]], exiting at either Kaizuka IC or Kishiwada-Izumi IC, and follow National Route 170. == References == {{Reflist}} {{Commons|Category:Ogami-jinja, Kishiwada}} {{Osaka 100 Best Green Sites}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{translated page|ja|意賀美神社 (岸和田市)|version=97795615|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shikinaisha in Izumi Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:Son-sha]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] }} [[sn:Okami Shrine (Kishiwada)]] [[sn:E6%84%8F%E8%B3%80%E7%BE%8E%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE (%E5%B2%B8%E5%92%8C%E7%94%B0%E5%B8%82)]] [[ja:&action=edit]] [[ja:意賀美神社]] [[Category:Son-sha in Osaka Prefecture]] {{Commons category|Ogami-jinja, Kishiwada}} [[ja:意賀美神社 (岸和田市)]] [[Category:paes with no wikidata]]

Buses run approximately twice per hour. - **By Car**: Use [[:en:Hanwa Expressway|Hanwa Expressway]], exiting at either Kaizuka IC or Kishiwada-Izumi IC, and follow National Route 170. == References == {{Reflist}} {{Commons|Category:Ogami-jinja, Kishiwada}} {{Osaka 100 Best Green Sites}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{translated page|ja|意賀美神社 (岸和田市)|version=97795615|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shikinaisha in Izumi Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:Son-sha]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] }} [[sn:Okami Shrine (Kishiwada)]] [[sn:E6%84%8F%E8%B3%80%E7%BE%8E%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE (%E5%B2%B8%E5%92%8C%E7%94%B0%E5%B8%82)]] [[Category:Redo these jawiki links]] [[ja:意賀美神社]] [[Category:Son-sha in Osaka Prefecture]] {{Commons category|Ogami-jinja, Kishiwada}} [[ja:意賀美神社 (岸和田市)]] [[Category:Pages with no Wikidata]]

{{afc comment|Wikidata entry (none) Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:44, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Shrine | name = Ogami Shrine (意賀美神社) | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:OgamiJinjaHaiden.jpg|250px|意賀美神社拝殿]]
Haiden {{Maplink2|zoom=12|frame=yes|plain=yes|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|frame-height=220|type=point|frame-latitude=34.437|frame-longitude=135.40|coord={{coord|34|24|57.54|N|135|24|38.94|E}}}} | location = 17 Habutaki-cho, Kishiwada City, Osaka Prefecture, Japan | coordinates = {{Coord|34|24|57.54|N|135|24|38.94|E|region:JP-27_scale:30000|display=inline,title}} | Deities = Yamigami-no-Ōkami
Ichikishima-hime-no-Mikoto
Hachidai Ryūō and Gozu Tennō
Itaki Iwa-no-Kami | Shrine_Rank = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} (Minor), Former Village Shrine | Foundation = Before the [[:en:Tenpyō|Tenpyō era]] (729–749) | Main_building = {{ill|Hafu-zukuri|ja|破風造|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hafu-zukuri|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} | Festival = October 5 }} '''Ogami Shrine''' (意賀美神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Kishiwada, Osaka|Kishiwada]], Osaka Prefecture, Japan. It is a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} (Minor Shrine) and was classified as a village shrine under the [[:en:Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines]]. The shrine serves as the tutelary deity for the former Amakawataki and Habutaki villages and is located at an important water management site. == History == During the reign of [[:en:Emperor Shōmu|Emperor Shōmu]], the shrine gained recognition in 732 CE (Tenpyō 4) for its efficacy in bringing rain during a severe drought. Similarly, during the reign of [[:en:Emperor Yōzei|Emperor Yōzei]] in 884 CE (Gangyō 8), {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|Sugawara no Michizane|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara no Michizane|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} offered prayers during another drought, and the shrine was revered for its rain-bringing miracles. Since then, the shrine has also been called the "Rainfall deity Shrine." On August 14, 1906 (Meiji 39), under the [[:en:Shrine Consolidation Policy|Shrine Consolidation Policy]] directive, the {{ill|Furumoto Shrine|ja|古元神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Furumoto Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and {{ill|Ichinose Shrine|ja|一瀬神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichinose Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, both located in Habutaki, were merged with Ogami Shrine on November 11, 1906. == Cultural Assets == * Shrine plaque inscribed by {{ill|Kazanin Atsunaga|ja|花山院厚卿|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kazanin Atsunaga|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} == Auxiliary Shrines == * Itsukushima Shrine * Ogami Kojin Shrine == Shrine Surroundings == The shrine is located at the northern foothills of {{ill|Kōnoyama|ja|神於山|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōnoyama|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. The site includes the "Rainfall Waterfall" (雨降りの滝), which flows into a tributary of the Tsudagawa River. Due to its inland location, the region often experiences reduced rainfall after the rainy season, similar to the Seto Inland Sea climate. However, the shrine's surroundings are notably humid, supporting diverse vegetation, including rare warm-temperate broadleaf trees. The forest around the shrine consists of evergreen broadleaf species dominated by {{ill|Castanopsis cuspidata|ja|コジイ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Castanopsis cuspidata|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ツブラジイ}} and {{ill|Elaeocarpus japonicus|ja|ミミズバイ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Elaeocarpus japonicus|qq=}}, along with [[:en:Elaeocarpus sylvestris|Holtunok]], {{ill|Ardisia japonica|ja|タイミンタチバナ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ardisia japonica|qq=}}, and {{ill|Corchoropsis tomentosa|ja|ツルコウジ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Corchoropsis tomentosa|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. This area was designated a Natural Monument by Kishiwada City in 1968 (Shōwa 43). It is also listed among the {{ill|Osaka 100 Best Green Sites|ja|大阪みどりの百選|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Osaka 100 Best Green Sites|qq=}} and was selected as an Osaka Prefecture Natural Conservation Area in 1989 (Heisei 1). However, the natural environment around the shrine, including Kōnoyama, has suffered significant degradation over the past 50 years. Restoration efforts are ongoing under the "Kōnoyama Area Natural Restoration Comprehensive Plan" (October 2004) and the "Kōnoyama Area Natural Restoration Project Implementation Plan" (June 2005), supervised by the Ministry of Agriculture, Forestry and Fisheries. == Access == - **By Bus**: From [[:en:Kishiwada Station|Kishiwada Station]] (Nankai Electric Railway) or [[:en:Higashi-Kishiwada Station|Higashi-Kishiwada Station]] (JR Hanwa Line), take the Nankai Wing Bus South on routes 0, 41, 42, or 43 to Miyano-dai. Buses run approximately twice per hour. - **By Car**: Use [[:en:Hanwa Expressway|Hanwa Expressway]], exiting at either Kaizuka IC or Kishiwada-Izumi IC, and follow National Route 170. == References == {{Reflist}} {{Commons|Category:Ogami-jinja, Kishiwada}} {{Osaka 100 Best Green Sites}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{translated page|ja|意賀美神社 (岸和田市)|version=97795615|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shikinaisha in Izumi Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:Son-sha]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] }} [[sn:Okami Shrine (Kishiwada)]] [[sn:E6%84%8F%E8%B3%80%E7%BE%8E%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE (%E5%B2%B8%E5%92%8C%E7%94%B0%E5%B8%82)]] [[ja:意賀美神社]] [[Category:Son-sha in Osaka Prefecture]] {{Commons category|Ogami-jinja, Kishiwada}} [[ja:意賀美神社 (岸和田市)]] [[Category:Pages with no Wikidata]]

{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11493038]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 05:24, 12 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry (none) Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:44, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Ogami Shrine (意賀美神社) | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:OgamiJinjaHaiden.jpg|250px|意賀美神社拝殿]]
Haiden {{Maplink2|zoom=12|frame=yes|plain=yes|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|frame-height=220|type=point|frame-latitude=34.437|frame-longitude=135.40|coord={{coord|34|24|57.54|N|135|24|38.94|E}}}} | location = 17 Habutaki-cho, Kishiwada City, Osaka Prefecture, Japan | coordinates = {{Coord|34|24|57.54|N|135|24|38.94|E|region:JP-27_scale:30000|display=inline,title}} | Deities = Yamigami-no-Ōkami
Ichikishima-hime-no-Mikoto
Hachidai Ryūō and Gozu Tennō
Itaki Iwa-no-Kami | Shrine_Rank = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} (Minor), Former Village Shrine | Foundation = Before the [[:en:Tenpyō|Tenpyō era]] (729–749) | Main_building = {{ill|Hafu-zukuri|ja|破風造|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hafu-zukuri|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} | Festival = October 5 }} '''Ogami Shrine''' (意賀美神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Kishiwada, Osaka|Kishiwada]], Osaka Prefecture, Japan. It is a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} (Minor Shrine) and was classified as a village shrine under the [[:en:Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines]]. The shrine serves as the tutelary deity for the former Amakawataki and Habutaki villages and is located at an important water management site. == History == During the reign of [[:en:Emperor Shōmu|Emperor Shōmu]], the shrine gained recognition in 732 CE (Tenpyō 4) for its efficacy in bringing rain during a severe drought. Similarly, during the reign of [[:en:Emperor Yōzei|Emperor Yōzei]] in 884 CE (Gangyō 8), {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|Sugawara no Michizane|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara no Michizane|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} offered prayers during another drought, and the shrine was revered for its rain-bringing miracles. Since then, the shrine has also been called the "Rainfall deity Shrine." On August 14, 1906 (Meiji 39), under the [[:en:Shrine Consolidation Policy|Shrine Consolidation Policy]] directive, the {{ill|Furumoto Shrine|ja|古元神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Furumoto Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and {{ill|Ichinose Shrine|ja|一瀬神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichinose Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, both located in Habutaki, were merged with Ogami Shrine on November 11, 1906. == Cultural Assets == * Shrine plaque inscribed by {{ill|Kazanin Atsunaga|ja|花山院厚卿|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kazanin Atsunaga|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} == Auxiliary Shrines == * Itsukushima Shrine * Ogami Kojin Shrine == Shrine Surroundings == The shrine is located at the northern foothills of {{ill|Kōnoyama|ja|神於山|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōnoyama|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. The site includes the "Rainfall Waterfall" (雨降りの滝), which flows into a tributary of the Tsudagawa River. Due to its inland location, the region often experiences reduced rainfall after the rainy season, similar to the Seto Inland Sea climate. However, the shrine's surroundings are notably humid, supporting diverse vegetation, including rare warm-temperate broadleaf trees. The forest around the shrine consists of evergreen broadleaf species dominated by {{ill|Castanopsis cuspidata|ja|コジイ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Castanopsis cuspidata|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ツブラジイ}} and {{ill|Elaeocarpus japonicus|ja|ミミズバイ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Elaeocarpus japonicus|qq=}}, along with [[:en:Elaeocarpus sylvestris|Holtunok]], {{ill|Ardisia japonica|ja|タイミンタチバナ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ardisia japonica|qq=}}, and {{ill|Corchoropsis tomentosa|ja|ツルコウジ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Corchoropsis tomentosa|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. This area was designated a Natural Monument by Kishiwada City in 1968 (Shōwa 43). It is also listed among the {{ill|Osaka 100 Best Green Sites|ja|大阪みどりの百選|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Osaka 100 Best Green Sites|qq=}} and was selected as an Osaka Prefecture Natural Conservation Area in 1989 (Heisei 1). However, the natural environment around the shrine, including Kōnoyama, has suffered significant degradation over the past 50 years. Restoration efforts are ongoing under the "Kōnoyama Area Natural Restoration Comprehensive Plan" (October 2004) and the "Kōnoyama Area Natural Restoration Project Implementation Plan" (June 2005), supervised by the Ministry of Agriculture, Forestry and Fisheries. == Access == - **By Bus**: From [[:en:Kishiwada Station|Kishiwada Station]] (Nankai Electric Railway) or [[:en:Higashi-Kishiwada Station|Higashi-Kishiwada Station]] (JR Hanwa Line), take the Nankai Wing Bus South on routes 0, 41, 42, or 43 to Miyano-dai. Buses run approximately twice per hour. - **By Car**: Use [[:en:Hanwa Expressway|Hanwa Expressway]], exiting at either Kaizuka IC or Kishiwada-Izumi IC, and follow National Route 170. == References == {{Reflist}} {{Commons|Category:Ogami-jinja, Kishiwada}} {{Osaka 100 Best Green Sites}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{translated page|ja|意賀美神社 (岸和田市)|version=97795615|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shikinaisha in Izumi Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:Son-sha]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] }} [[sn:Okami Shrine (Kishiwada)]] [[sn:E6%84%8F%E8%B3%80%E7%BE%8E%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE (%E5%B2%B8%E5%92%8C%E7%94%B0%E5%B8%82)]] [[ja:意賀美神社]] [[Category:Son-sha in Osaka Prefecture]] {{Commons category|Ogami-jinja, Kishiwada}} [[ja:意賀美神社 (岸和田市)]] [[Category:pages without english wikidata name]]

Ichikishima-hime-no-Mikoto
Hachidai Ryūō and Gozu Tennō
Itaki Iwa-no-Kami | Shrine_Rank = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} (Minor), Former Village Shrine | Foundation = Before the [[:en:Tenpyō|Tenpyō era]] (729–749) | Main_building = {{ill|Hafu-zukuri|ja|破風造|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hafu-zukuri|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} | Festival = October 5 }} '''Ogami Shrine''' (意賀美神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Kishiwada, Osaka|Kishiwada]], Osaka Prefecture, Japan. It is a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} (Minor Shrine) and was classified as a village shrine under the [[:en:Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines]]. The shrine serves as the tutelary deity for the former Amakawataki and Habutaki villages and is located at an important water management site. == History == During the reign of [[:en:Emperor Shōmu|Emperor Shōmu]], the shrine gained recognition in 732 CE (Tenpyō 4) for its efficacy in bringing rain during a severe drought. Similarly, during the reign of [[:en:Emperor Yōzei|Emperor Yōzei]] in 884 CE (Gangyō 8), {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|ja|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara no Michizane|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q382005}} offered prayers during another drought, and the shrine was revered for its rain-bringing miracles. Since then, the shrine has also been called the "Rainfall deity Shrine." On August 14, 1906 (Meiji 39), under the [[:en:Shrine Consolidation Policy|Shrine Consolidation Policy]] directive, the {{ill|Furumoto Shrine|ja|古元神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Furumoto Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and {{ill|Ichinose Shrine|ja|一瀬神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichinose Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, both located in Habutaki, were merged with Ogami Shrine on November 11, 1906. == Cultural Assets == * Shrine plaque inscribed by {{ill|Kazanin Atsunaga|ja|花山院厚卿|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kazanin Atsunaga|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} == Auxiliary Shrines == * Itsukushima Shrine * Ogami Kojin Shrine == Shrine Surroundings == The shrine is located at the northern foothills of {{ill|Kōnoyama|ja|神於山|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōnoyama|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. The site includes the "Rainfall Waterfall" (雨降りの滝), which flows into a tributary of the Tsudagawa River. Due to its inland location, the region often experiences reduced rainfall after the rainy season, similar to the Seto Inland Sea climate. However, the shrine's surroundings are notably humid, supporting diverse vegetation, including rare warm-temperate broadleaf trees. The forest around the shrine consists of evergreen broadleaf species dominated by {{ill|Castanopsis cuspidata|ja|コジイ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Castanopsis cuspidata|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ツブラジイ|1=Castanopsis cuspidata|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2717620}} and {{ill|Elaeocarpus japonicus|ja|ミミズバイ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Elaeocarpus japonicus|qq=|1=Symplocos glauca|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q15235283}}, along with [[:en:Elaeocarpus sylvestris|Holtunok]], {{ill|Ardisia japonica|ja|タイミンタチバナ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ardisia japonica|qq=|1=Rapanea neriifolia|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10953457}}, and {{ill|Corchoropsis tomentosa|ja|ツルコウジ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Corchoropsis tomentosa|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. This area was designated a Natural Monument by Kishiwada City in 1968 (Shōwa 43). It is also listed among the {{ill|Osaka 100 Best Green Sites|ja|大阪みどりの百選|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Osaka 100 Best Green Sites|qq=|1=Ōsaka Midori no Hyakusen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11439936}} and was selected as an Osaka Prefecture Natural Conservation Area in 1989 (Heisei 1). However, the natural environment around the shrine, including Kōnoyama, has suffered significant degradation over the past 50 years. Restoration efforts are ongoing under the "Kōnoyama Area Natural Restoration Comprehensive Plan" (October 2004) and the "Kōnoyama Area Natural Restoration Project Implementation Plan" (June 2005), supervised by the Ministry of Agriculture, Forestry and Fisheries. == Access == - **By Bus**: From [[:en:Kishiwada Station|Kishiwada Station]] (Nankai Electric Railway) or [[:en:Higashi-Kishiwada Station|Higashi-Kishiwada Station]] (JR Hanwa Line), take the Nankai Wing Bus South on routes 0, 41, 42, or 43 to Miyano-dai. Buses run approximately twice per hour. - **By Car**: Use [[:en:Hanwa Expressway|Hanwa Expressway]], exiting at either Kaizuka IC or Kishiwada-Izumi IC, and follow National Route 170. == References == {{Reflist}} {{Commons|Category:Ogami-jinja, Kishiwada}} {{Osaka 100 Best Green Sites}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{translated page|ja|意賀美神社 (岸和田市)|version=97795615|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shikinaisha in Izumi Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:Son-sha]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] }} [[sn:Okami Shrine (Kishiwada)]] [[sn:E6%84%8F%E8%B3%80%E7%BE%8E%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE (%E5%B2%B8%E5%92%8C%E7%94%B0%E5%B8%82)]] [[ja:意賀美神社]] [[Category:Son-sha in Osaka Prefecture]] {{Commons category|Ogami-jinja, Kishiwada}} [[ja:意賀美神社 (岸和田市)]] [[Category:pages without english wikidata name]]

{{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11493038]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 05:24, 12 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{{afc comment|Wikidata entry (none) Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:44, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Ogami Shrine (意賀美神社) | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:OgamiJinjaHaiden.jpg|250px|意賀美神社拝殿]]
Haiden {{Maplink2|zoom=12|frame=yes|plain=yes|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|frame-height=220|type=point|frame-latitude=34.437|frame-longitude=135.40|coord={{coord|34|24|57.54|N|135|24|38.94|E}}}} | location = 17 Habutaki-cho, Kishiwada City, Osaka Prefecture, Japan | coordinates = {{Coord|34|24|57.54|N|135|24|38.94|E|region:JP-27_scale:30000|display=inline,title}} | Deities = Yamigami-no-Ōkami
Ichikishima-hime-no-Mikoto
Hachidai Ryūō and Gozu Tennō
Itaki Iwa-no-Kami | Shrine_Rank = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} (Minor), Former Village Shrine | Foundation = Before the [[:en:Tenpyō|Tenpyō era]] (729–749) | Main_building = {{ill|Hafu-zukuri|ja|破風造|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hafu-zukuri|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} | Festival = October 5 }} '''Ogami Shrine''' (意賀美神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Kishiwada, Osaka|Kishiwada]], Osaka Prefecture, Japan. It is a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} (Minor Shrine) and was classified as a village shrine under the [[:en:Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines]]. The shrine serves as the tutelary deity for the former Amakawataki and Habutaki villages and is located at an important water management site. == History == During the reign of [[:en:Emperor Shōmu|Emperor Shōmu]], the shrine gained recognition in 732 CE (Tenpyō 4) for its efficacy in bringing rain during a severe drought. Similarly, during the reign of [[:en:Emperor Yōzei|Emperor Yōzei]] in 884 CE (Gangyō 8), {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|ja|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara no Michizane|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q382005}} offered prayers during another drought, and the shrine was revered for its rain-bringing miracles. Since then, the shrine has also been called the "Rainfall deity Shrine." On August 14, 1906 (Meiji 39), under the [[:en:Shrine Consolidation Policy|Shrine Consolidation Policy]] directive, the {{ill|Furumoto Shrine|ja|古元神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Furumoto Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and {{ill|Ichinose Shrine|ja|一瀬神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichinose Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, both located in Habutaki, were merged with Ogami Shrine on November 11, 1906. == Cultural Assets == * Shrine plaque inscribed by {{ill|Kazanin Atsunaga|ja|花山院厚卿|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kazanin Atsunaga|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} == Auxiliary Shrines == * Itsukushima Shrine * Ogami Kojin Shrine == Shrine Surroundings == The shrine is located at the northern foothills of {{ill|Kōnoyama|ja|神於山|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōnoyama|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. The site includes the "Rainfall Waterfall" (雨降りの滝), which flows into a tributary of the Tsudagawa River. Due to its inland location, the region often experiences reduced rainfall after the rainy season, similar to the Seto Inland Sea climate. However, the shrine's surroundings are notably humid, supporting diverse vegetation, including rare warm-temperate broadleaf trees. The forest around the shrine consists of evergreen broadleaf species dominated by {{ill|Castanopsis cuspidata|ja|コジイ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Castanopsis cuspidata|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ツブラジイ|1=Castanopsis cuspidata|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2717620}} and {{ill|Elaeocarpus japonicus|ja|ミミズバイ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Elaeocarpus japonicus|qq=|1=Symplocos glauca|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q15235283}}, along with [[:en:Elaeocarpus sylvestris|Holtunok]], {{ill|Ardisia japonica|ja|タイミンタチバナ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ardisia japonica|qq=|1=Rapanea neriifolia|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10953457}}, and {{ill|Corchoropsis tomentosa|ja|ツルコウジ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Corchoropsis tomentosa|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. This area was designated a Natural Monument by Kishiwada City in 1968 (Shōwa 43). It is also listed among the {{ill|Osaka 100 Best Green Sites|ja|大阪みどりの百選|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Osaka 100 Best Green Sites|qq=|1=Ōsaka Midori no Hyakusen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11439936}} and was selected as an Osaka Prefecture Natural Conservation Area in 1989 (Heisei 1). However, the natural environment around the shrine, including Kōnoyama, has suffered significant degradation over the past 50 years. Restoration efforts are ongoing under the "Kōnoyama Area Natural Restoration Comprehensive Plan" (October 2004) and the "Kōnoyama Area Natural Restoration Project Implementation Plan" (June 2005), supervised by the Ministry of Agriculture, Forestry and Fisheries. == Access == - **By Bus**: From [[:en:Kishiwada Station|Kishiwada Station]] (Nankai Electric Railway) or [[:en:Higashi-Kishiwada Station|Higashi-Kishiwada Station]] (JR Hanwa Line), take the Nankai Wing Bus South on routes 0, 41, 42, or 43 to Miyano-dai. Buses run approximately twice per hour. - **By Car**: Use [[:en:Hanwa Expressway|Hanwa Expressway]], exiting at either Kaizuka IC or Kishiwada-Izumi IC, and follow National Route 170. == References == {{Reflist}} {{Commons|Category:Ogami-jinja, Kishiwada}} {{Osaka 100 Best Green Sites}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{translated page|ja|意賀美神社 (岸和田市)|version=97795615|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shikinaisha in Izumi Province]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Okami Shrine (Kishiwada)]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:Son-sha]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] }} [[sn:Okami Shrine (Kishiwada)]] [[sn:E6%84%8F%E8%B3%80%E7%BE%8E%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE (%E5%B2%B8%E5%92%8C%E7%94%B0%E5%B8%82)]] [[ja:意賀美神社]] [[Category:Son-sha in Osaka Prefecture]] {{Commons category|Ogami-jinja, Kishiwada}} [[ja:意賀美神社 (岸和田市)]] [[Category:pages without english wikidata name]] [[Category:qq]]

Buses run approximately twice per hour. - **By Car**: Use [[:en:Hanwa Expressway|Hanwa Expressway]], exiting at either Kaizuka IC or Kishiwada-Izumi IC, and follow National Route 170. == References == {{Reflist}} {{Commons|Category:Ogami-jinja, Kishiwada}} {{Osaka 100 Best Green Sites}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{translated page|ja|意賀美神社 (岸和田市)|version=97795615|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shikinaisha in Izumi Province]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Okami Shrine (Kishiwada)]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:Son-sha]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] }} [[sn:Okami Shrine (Kishiwada)]] [[sn:E6%84%8F%E8%B3%80%E7%BE%8E%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE (%E5%B2%B8%E5%92%8C%E7%94%B0%E5%B8%82)]] [[ja:意賀美神社]] [[Category:Son-sha in Osaka Prefecture]] {{Commons category|Ogami-jinja, Kishiwada}} [[ja:意賀美神社 (岸和田市)]] [[Category:pages without english wikidata name]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]]

revision.text:
{{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11493038]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 05:24, 12 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{{afc comment|Wikidata entry (none) Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:44, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Ogami Shrine (意賀美神社) | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:OgamiJinjaHaiden.jpg|250px|意賀美神社拝殿]]
Haiden {{Maplink2|zoom=12|frame=yes|plain=yes|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|frame-height=220|type=point|frame-latitude=34.437|frame-longitude=135.40|coord={{coord|34|24|57.54|N|135|24|38.94|E}}}} | location = 17 Habutaki-cho, Kishiwada City, Osaka Prefecture, Japan | coordinates = {{Coord|34|24|57.54|N|135|24|38.94|E|region:JP-27_scale:30000|display=inline,title}} | Deities = Yamigami-no-Ōkami
Ichikishima-hime-no-Mikoto
Hachidai Ryūō and Gozu Tennō
Itaki Iwa-no-Kami | Shrine_Rank = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} (Minor), Former Village Shrine | Foundation = Before the [[:en:Tenpyō|Tenpyō era]] (729–749) | Main_building = {{ill|Hafu-zukuri|ja|破風造|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hafu-zukuri|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} | Festival = October 5 }} '''Ogami Shrine''' (意賀美神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Kishiwada, Osaka|Kishiwada]], Osaka Prefecture, Japan. It is a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} (Minor Shrine) and was classified as a village shrine under the [[:en:Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines]]. The shrine serves as the tutelary deity for the former Amakawataki and Habutaki villages and is located at an important water management site. == History == During the reign of [[:en:Emperor Shōmu|Emperor Shōmu]], the shrine gained recognition in 732 CE (Tenpyō 4) for its efficacy in bringing rain during a severe drought. Similarly, during the reign of [[:en:Emperor Yōzei|Emperor Yōzei]] in 884 CE (Gangyō 8), {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|ja|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara no Michizane|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q382005}} offered prayers during another drought, and the shrine was revered for its rain-bringing miracles. Since then, the shrine has also been called the "Rainfall deity Shrine." On August 14, 1906 (Meiji 39), under the [[:en:Shrine Consolidation Policy|Shrine Consolidation Policy]] directive, the {{ill|Furumoto Shrine|ja|古元神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Furumoto Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and {{ill|Ichinose Shrine|ja|一瀬神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichinose Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, both located in Habutaki, were merged with Ogami Shrine on November 11, 1906. == Cultural Assets == * Shrine plaque inscribed by {{ill|Kazanin Atsunaga|ja|花山院厚卿|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kazanin Atsunaga|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} == Auxiliary Shrines == * Itsukushima Shrine * Ogami Kojin Shrine == Shrine Surroundings == The shrine is located at the northern foothills of {{ill|Kōnoyama|ja|神於山|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōnoyama|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. The site includes the "Rainfall Waterfall" (雨降りの滝), which flows into a tributary of the Tsudagawa River. Due to its inland location, the region often experiences reduced rainfall after the rainy season, similar to the Seto Inland Sea climate. However, the shrine's surroundings are notably humid, supporting diverse vegetation, including rare warm-temperate broadleaf trees. The forest around the shrine consists of evergreen broadleaf species dominated by {{ill|Castanopsis cuspidata|ja|コジイ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Castanopsis cuspidata|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ツブラジイ|1=Castanopsis cuspidata|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2717620}} and {{ill|Elaeocarpus japonicus|ja|ミミズバイ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Elaeocarpus japonicus|qq=|1=Symplocos glauca|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q15235283}}, along with [[:en:Elaeocarpus sylvestris|Holtunok]], {{ill|Ardisia japonica|ja|タイミンタチバナ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ardisia japonica|qq=|1=Rapanea neriifolia|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10953457}}, and {{ill|Corchoropsis tomentosa|ja|ツルコウジ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Corchoropsis tomentosa|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. This area was designated a Natural Monument by Kishiwada City in 1968 (Shōwa 43). It is also listed among the {{ill|Osaka 100 Best Green Sites|ja|大阪みどりの百選|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Osaka 100 Best Green Sites|qq=|1=Ōsaka Midori no Hyakusen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11439936}} and was selected as an Osaka Prefecture Natural Conservation Area in 1989 (Heisei 1). However, the natural environment around the shrine, including Kōnoyama, has suffered significant degradation over the past 50 years. Restoration efforts are ongoing under the "Kōnoyama Area Natural Restoration Comprehensive Plan" (October 2004) and the "Kōnoyama Area Natural Restoration Project Implementation Plan" (June 2005), supervised by the Ministry of Agriculture, Forestry and Fisheries. == Access == - **By Bus**: From [[:en:Kishiwada Station|Kishiwada Station]] (Nankai Electric Railway) or [[:en:Higashi-Kishiwada Station|Higashi-Kishiwada Station]] (JR Hanwa Line), take the Nankai Wing Bus South on routes 0, 41, 42, or 43 to Miyano-dai. Buses run approximately twice per hour. - **By Car**: Use [[:en:Hanwa Expressway|Hanwa Expressway]], exiting at either Kaizuka IC or Kishiwada-Izumi IC, and follow National Route 170. == References == {{Reflist}} {{Commons|Category:Ogami-jinja, Kishiwada}} {{Osaka 100 Best Green Sites}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{translated page|ja|意賀美神社 (岸和田市)|version=97795615|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shikinaisha in Izumi Province]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Okami Shrine (Kishiwada)]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:Son-sha]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] }} [[sn:Okami Shrine (Kishiwada)]] [[sn:E6%84%8F%E8%B3%80%E7%BE%8E%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE (%E5%B2%B8%E5%92%8C%E7%94%B0%E5%B8%82)]] [[ja:意賀美神社]] [[Category:Son-sha in Osaka Prefecture]] {{Commons category|Ogami-jinja, Kishiwada}} [[ja:意賀美神社 (岸和田市)]] [[Category:pages without english wikidata name]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]]

Due to its inland location, the region often experiences reduced rainfall after the rainy season, similar to the Seto Inland Sea climate. However, the shrine's surroundings are notably humid, supporting diverse vegetation, including rare warm-temperate broadleaf trees. The forest around the shrine consists of evergreen broadleaf species dominated by {{ill|Castanopsis cuspidata|ja|コジイ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Castanopsis cuspidata|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ツブラジイ|1=Castanopsis cuspidata|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2717620}} and {{ill|Elaeocarpus japonicus|ja|ミミズバイ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Elaeocarpus japonicus|qq=|1=Symplocos glauca|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q15235283}}, along with [[:en:Elaeocarpus sylvestris|Holtunok]], {{ill|Ardisia japonica|ja|タイミンタチバナ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ardisia japonica|qq=|1=Rapanea neriifolia|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10953457}}, and {{ill|Corchoropsis tomentosa|ja|ツルコウジ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Corchoropsis tomentosa|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. This area was designated a Natural Monument by Kishiwada City in 1968 (Shōwa 43). It is also listed among the {{ill|Osaka 100 Best Green Sites|ja|大阪みどりの百選|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Osaka 100 Best Green Sites|qq=|1=Ōsaka Midori no Hyakusen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11439936}} and was selected as an Osaka Prefecture Natural Conservation Area in 1989 (Heisei 1). However, the natural environment around the shrine, including Kōnoyama, has suffered significant degradation over the past 50 years. Restoration efforts are ongoing under the "Kōnoyama Area Natural Restoration Comprehensive Plan" (October 2004) and the "Kōnoyama Area Natural Restoration Project Implementation Plan" (June 2005), supervised by the Ministry of Agriculture, Forestry and Fisheries. == Access == - **By Bus**: From [[:en:Kishiwada Station|Kishiwada Station]] (Nankai Electric Railway) or [[:en:Higashi-Kishiwada Station|Higashi-Kishiwada Station]] (JR Hanwa Line), take the Nankai Wing Bus South on routes 0, 41, 42, or 43 to Miyano-dai. Buses run approximately twice per hour. - **By Car**: Use [[:en:Hanwa Expressway|Hanwa Expressway]], exiting at either Kaizuka IC or Kishiwada-Izumi IC, and follow National Route 170. == References == {{Reflist}} {{Commons|Category:Ogami-jinja, Kishiwada}} {{Osaka 100 Best Green Sites}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{translated page|ja|意賀美神社 (岸和田市)|version=97795615|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shikinaisha in Izumi Province]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Okami Shrine (Kishiwada)]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:Son-sha]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] }} [[sn:Okami Shrine (Kishiwada)]] [[sn:E6%84%8F%E8%B3%80%E7%BE%8E%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE (%E5%B2%B8%E5%92%8C%E7%94%B0%E5%B8%82)]] [[ja:意賀美神社]] [[Category:Son-sha in Osaka Prefecture]] {{Commons category|Ogami-jinja, Kishiwada}} [[ja:意賀美神社 (岸和田市)]] [[Category:pages without english wikidata name]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]]

{{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11493038]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 05:24, 12 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{{afc comment|Wikidata entry (none) Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:44, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Ogami Shrine (意賀美神社) | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:OgamiJinjaHaiden.jpg|250px|意賀美神社拝殿]]
Haiden {{Maplink2|zoom=12|frame=yes|plain=yes|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|frame-height=220|type=point|frame-latitude=34.437|frame-longitude=135.40|coord={{coord|34|24|57.54|N|135|24|38.94|E}}}} | location = 17 Habutaki-cho, Kishiwada City, Osaka Prefecture, Japan | coordinates = {{Coord|34|24|57.54|N|135|24|38.94|E|region:JP-27_scale:30000|display=inline,title}} | Deities = Yamigami-no-Ōkami
Ichikishima-hime-no-Mikoto
Hachidai Ryūō and Gozu Tennō
Itaki Iwa-no-Kami | Shrine_Rank = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} (Minor), Former Village Shrine | Foundation = Before the [[:en:Tenpyō|Tenpyō era]] (729–749) | Main_building = {{ill|Hafu-zukuri|ja|破風造|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hafu-zukuri|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} | Festival = October 5 }} '''Ogami Shrine''' (意賀美神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Kishiwada, Osaka|Kishiwada]], Osaka Prefecture, Japan. It is a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} (Minor Shrine) and was classified as a village shrine under the [[:en:Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines]]. The shrine serves as the tutelary deity for the former Amakawataki and Habutaki villages and is located at an important water management site. == History == During the reign of [[:en:Emperor Shōmu|Emperor Shōmu]], the shrine gained recognition in 732 CE (Tenpyō 4) for its efficacy in bringing rain during a severe drought. Similarly, during the reign of [[:en:Emperor Yōzei|Emperor Yōzei]] in 884 CE (Gangyō 8), {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|ja|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara no Michizane|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q382005}} offered prayers during another drought, and the shrine was revered for its rain-bringing miracles. Since then, the shrine has also been called the "Rainfall deity Shrine." On August 14, 1906 (Meiji 39), under the [[:en:Shrine Consolidation Policy|Shrine Consolidation Policy]] directive, the {{ill|Furumoto Shrine|ja|古元神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Furumoto Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and {{ill|Ichinose Shrine|ja|一瀬神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichinose Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, both located in Habutaki, were merged with Ogami Shrine on November 11, 1906. == Cultural Assets == * Shrine plaque inscribed by {{ill|Kazanin Atsunaga|ja|花山院厚卿|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kazanin Atsunaga|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} == Auxiliary Shrines == * Itsukushima Shrine * Ogami Kojin Shrine == Shrine Surroundings == The shrine is located at the northern foothills of {{ill|Kōnoyama|ja|神於山|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōnoyama|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. The site includes the "Rainfall Waterfall" (雨降りの滝), which flows into a tributary of the Tsudagawa River. Due to its inland location, the region often experiences reduced rainfall after the rainy season, similar to the Seto Inland Sea climate. However, the shrine's surroundings are notably humid, supporting diverse vegetation, including rare warm-temperate broadleaf trees. The forest around the shrine consists of evergreen broadleaf species dominated by {{ill|Castanopsis cuspidata|ja|コジイ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Castanopsis cuspidata|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ツブラジイ|1=Castanopsis cuspidata|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2717620}} and {{ill|Elaeocarpus japonicus|ja|ミミズバイ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Elaeocarpus japonicus|qq=|1=Symplocos glauca|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q15235283}}, along with [[:en:Elaeocarpus sylvestris|Holtunok]], {{ill|Ardisia japonica|ja|タイミンタチバナ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ardisia japonica|qq=|1=Rapanea neriifolia|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10953457}}, and {{ill|Corchoropsis tomentosa|ja|ツルコウジ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Corchoropsis tomentosa|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. This area was designated a Natural Monument by Kishiwada City in 1968 (Shōwa 43). It is also listed among the {{ill|Osaka 100 Best Green Sites|ja|大阪みどりの百選|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Osaka 100 Best Green Sites|qq=|1=Ōsaka Midori no Hyakusen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11439936}} and was selected as an Osaka Prefecture Natural Conservation Area in 1989 (Heisei 1). However, the natural environment around the shrine, including Kōnoyama, has suffered significant degradation over the past 50 years. Restoration efforts are ongoing under the "Kōnoyama Area Natural Restoration Comprehensive Plan" (October 2004) and the "Kōnoyama Area Natural Restoration Project Implementation Plan" (June 2005), supervised by the Ministry of Agriculture, Forestry and Fisheries. == Access == - **By Bus**: From [[:en:Kishiwada Station|Kishiwada Station]] (Nankai Electric Railway) or [[:en:Higashi-Kishiwada Station|Higashi-Kishiwada Station]] (JR Hanwa Line), take the Nankai Wing Bus South on routes 0, 41, 42, or 43 to Miyano-dai. Buses run approximately twice per hour. - **By Car**: Use [[:en:Hanwa Expressway|Hanwa Expressway]], exiting at either Kaizuka IC or Kishiwada-Izumi IC, and follow National Route 170. == References == {{Reflist}} {{Commons|Category:Ogami-jinja, Kishiwada}} {{Osaka 100 Best Green Sites}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{translated page|ja|意賀美神社 (岸和田市)|version=97795615|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shikinaisha in Izumi Province]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Okami Shrine (Kishiwada)]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:Son-sha]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] }} [[sn:Okami Shrine (Kishiwada)]] [[sn:E6%84%8F%E8%B3%80%E7%BE%8E%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE (%E5%B2%B8%E5%92%8C%E7%94%B0%E5%B8%82)]] [[ja:意賀美神社]] [[Category:Son-sha in Osaka Prefecture]] {{Commons category|Ogami-jinja, Kishiwada}} [[ja:意賀美神社 (岸和田市)]] [[Category:pages without english wikidata name]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Okami Shrine (Kishiwada)]][[Category:qq]]
